races.race_name: Watch On Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Elemental Eye
runners.spotlight: Two wins in the second half of 2025, the latest a wide-margin success here over 1m6f (soft); Sunday's return to action at Ripon should have sharpened him up but others are still more appealing for win purposes.
runners.comment: Course win last October (1m6f, soft); subsequent efforts less persuasive

runners.name: Ravenswell
runners.spotlight: Had a near miss at Carlisle (1m3f, good to firm) last August and not beaten far into fourth at Newcastle nine days later; not so good twice after and he has his stamina to prove now returning from 237 days off.
runners.comment: Should have a small race in him but he returns from 237 days off with stamina to prove

runners.name: Gallus Norman
runners.spotlight: Struggled in bumpers/maiden and novice races on his first five starts but the switch to middle-distance handicaps sparked a good jolt of improvement three weeks ago, finding only the red-hot favourite too strong over 1m4f here; 2m should be within range and he has a top amateur booked; obvious chance of going one better.
runners.comment: Good step forward when second on handicap debut here last month (1m4f); more to come at 2m

runners.name: Bronte Beach
runners.spotlight: Well-bred filly; took a while to get the hang of things but she took a good step forward when upped to 1m6f and fitted with a hood at Southwell three weeks ago, running out the 7l winner of a 0-55 event; up 10lb and has another 2f to travel today but surprising if she has reached her ceiling yet.
runners.comment: Upped to 1m6 last time and responded with wide-margin success; up 10lb but not yet exposed

runners.name: Zimmerman
runners.spotlight: Solid effort when second of eight at Catterick ten days ago (2m, good; first run in this combination of accessories); should remain competitive.
runners.comment: Solid second at Catterick (2m, good) ten days ago; should remain competitive

runners.name: Yurinov
runners.spotlight: 0-27; the odd encouraging effort since joining Philip Kirby but he needs to up his game to trouble some of these rivals.
runners.comment: 27-race maiden; snippets of form to give him a squeak but still opposable for the win

runners.name: Star Of Markinch
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden; effective at the track and ran creditably over 1m4f here last week; still looks opposable for win purposes.
runners.comment: Exposed low-grade maiden; minor money her best hope once again

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cailin Aine
runners.spotlight: Promise in her first two runs, notably from a wide stall in the Lily Agnes at Chester, but she took a backwards step when fourth of 12 at Thirsk last week (13-8f); solid contender if bouncing back to her best.
runners.comment: Her first two runs were promising but she disappointed as favourite at Thirsk last week

runners.name: Anna Belardo
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise in two novice runs last month (5f, good and 6f, AW); quick enough to cope with the drop back to 5f and should go well, for all he isn't ideally drawn.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise in two runs last month; can do better but not ideally drawn

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: Not progressed from his debut effort (C&D) in two subsequent runs but this should be easier than Chester last time, a race where the leaders went off too hard; stable also represented by Cailin Aine.
runners.comment: Not progressing but had an excuse last time and this should be easier; trainer runs two

runners.name: Autumn Jewel
runners.spotlight: 6,000gns yearling; third foal of a maiden half-sister to two useful winners; hooded for debut; stable yet to strike with 2yos this year.
runners.comment: 6,000gns yearling; dam a maiden from a good family; yard's 2yos yet to strike in 2026

runners.name: Time And Effort
runners.spotlight: Not long turned two (May 11 foal); £5,000 yearling; ninth foal; half-sister to three winners out of a minor 2yo winner; stable's modest strike-rate with 2yos doesn't point to her being the answer today.
runners.comment: £5,000 half-sister to 3 winners out of a minor sprint winner; yard has modest 2yo record

runners.name: Mick's The Boy
runners.spotlight: 3,000gns yearling; eighth foal; half-brother to 5f/7f 2yo winner Gray's Inn (RPR 88); dam unraced sister to Listed-placed 10.5f/1m4f winner Diyaraka; stable's newcomers usually better for a run.
runners.comment: 3,000gns half-brother to 5f/7f 2yo winner Gray's Inn; stable's newcomers better for a run

runners.name: Explainingislosing
runners.spotlight: Easy to back and couldn't land a telling blow on his Beverley debut (5f, good to soft) 17 days ago, but he was keeping on at the finish and should be more streetwise with that run behind him; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Green when unable to land telling blow at Beverley on debut; should fare better this time

runners.name: Stay On Tab
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two C&D runs last month; nurseries should prove more suitable in time.
runners.comment: Modest form in two C&D runs last month; nurseries should prove more suitable in time

races.race_name: Watermans Straightforward Legal Advice Restricted Maiden Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C & D) (GBB)

runners.name: Whernside
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Newcastle on his 2yo debut (1m) but he has run well in defeat in two runs this spring, latterly when second of five on turf debut at Redcar three weeks ago (7f, good to firm); should go well again.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 7f twice last month, including on turf debut; solid contender
runners.owner: Unique Retreats Stays Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Blakefell
runners.spotlight: Faced some stiff tasks as a 2yo and performed with credit in sales races at both Doncaster and Newmarket; second over C&D (warm favourite) on his final 2yo start, after which he underwent wind surgery; returns at a realistic level; should go well.
runners.comment: Clear promise as a 2yo, including over C&D; had wind op since last seen; can go well
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services Ltd & Graeme Curnow

runners.name: Star Banner
runners.spotlight: 38,000euros yearling; second foal of a 6f 2yo winner (RPR 91); in good hands and he won't need to have star quality to play a part on debut.
runners.comment: 38,000euros yearling; dam a 6f 2yo winner (RPR 91); no surprise to see him feature

runners.name: You Will See
runners.spotlight: Outran his 33-1 odds when second behind a ready winner at Southwell last month (7f); open to improvement but others have achieved more.
runners.comment: Outran 33-1 odds when 2nd to ready winner at Southwell on last month's debut (7f); chance

runners.name: Dream Odyssey
runners.spotlight: 50-1 for last month's Thirsk debut (7f, good) and shaped with encouragement amidst evident inexperience off the bridle; open to improvement and no surprise to see him step forward.
runners.comment: Ran okay when a 50-1 shot for a Thirsk maiden (7f, good) last month; capable of better

races.race_name: Fridge Spares Wholesale Limited Handicap

runners.name: Krissy
runners.spotlight: Something clicked over the winter and she has made good progress since springing a 40-1 shock at Newcastle in November (1m4f), adding further wins at Wolverhampton and over C&D; ran as well as she ever has when a close second at Hamilton on Sunday (1m3f); that was a Class 4 and she should be hard to beat back down to Class 6 company.
runners.comment: Progressive mare; C&D win last month was followed by Class 4 2nd on Sunday; strong claims

runners.name: On The Bubble
runners.spotlight: Still seeking her first win for this yard but she has often run well, including over C&D, and her second here three weeks ago was on the back of a 190-day layoff; that form has been franked and she should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Conditions shouldn't faze her and her reappearance 2nd has been franked; one to consider

runners.name: Samra Star
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner on AW but unplaced in all seven turf runs, the latest of which was over C&D last month; not a solid option.
runners.comment: Three-time AW winner but she doees not have comparable form on grass

runners.name: Misemerald
runners.spotlight: Without a win since July 2023; didn't run badly at Newcastle last month but it remains to be seen if that can be a springboard to better things.
runners.comment: On a losing run and she isn't certain to build on the promise of her latest run (1m, AW)

runners.name: Pebble Dash
runners.spotlight: Ran well twice on AW in March but he suffered a heavy defeat at Newcastle last month; steps up in trip with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Below par last time but two solid AW runs in March; steps up in trip with stamina to prove

runners.name: Falcon Nine
runners.spotlight: Won twice in the space of five days last August, the second win coming over C&D off today's mark; ran some fair races on AW over the winter; place claims back from four months off.
runners.comment: Two wins in five days last August, including C&D; place claims back from four months off

runners.name: Glasses Up
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has won at this track but who is better known for his exploits at Ayr; solid third at that track 16 days ago (1m1f, good to firm); shouldn't be far away if backing it up.
runners.comment: Veteran who ran well for third at his beloved Ayr 16 days ago; chance with a repeat

races.race_name: Never Standing Still Handicap

runners.name: Kosometsuke
runners.spotlight: Game effort to win a 7f handicap at Thirsk on his seasonal and stable debut last month; only fifth back at Thirsk this month but the drop to 6f may have counted against him; not discounted.
runners.comment: 7f win on stable debut last month; not so good over 6f latest; may bounce back

runners.name: Diamondonthehill
runners.spotlight: Not an easy horse to win with but he had a solid AW campaign and is on a feasible mark; below par back on turf 12 days ago and needs to bounce back quickly.
runners.comment: Solid AW campaign this winter but he wasn't at his best returned to turf 12 days ago

runners.name: Jannas Journey
runners.spotlight: Yet to find her form this year but she has dropped 6lb in the weights and this C&D winner is just 2lb higher than for her last win; well held in the 3.42 Ayr Wednesday.
runners.comment: C&D winner; quiet start to 2026 but dropping in the weights; well held in 3.42 Wednesday

runners.name: Benacre
runners.spotlight: Front-running third over C&D last month; ridden more patiently when only fifth behind Persuasion here 25 days ago; up in class and may prove vulnerable for win purposes again.
runners.comment: Solid efforts over C&D on his last two starts but more will be needed in this higher grade

runners.name: Bellarchi
runners.spotlight: Her last four wins have come at this track, with two of them (7f/1m) last month; drawn wide when down the field at Chester two weeks ago but she has fared better on that front today; big run on the cards.
runners.comment: Good course record; poor draw when down the field at Chester last time; leading contender

runners.name: Gweedore
runners.spotlight: Stable stalwart; just 1lb higher than for his front-running C&D win last November (soft) but his two runs this year have been unconvincing; will no doubt pop up at some point but perhaps in a race with less pace pressure.
runners.comment: Fine servant to the yard and conditions hold no terrors; not shone in two runs this spring

runners.name: Persuasion
runners.spotlight: Got up late to win a C&D handicap last month, his second course win; only midfield at Thirsk last week and minor money perhaps his best hope.
runners.comment: Led late in C&D handicap last month; only midfield at Thirsk on Saturday; others stronger

runners.name: Misunderstood
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner in France as a 2yo but lost his form in 2025; weak in market and beat just one home at Newmarket on reappearance/stable debut; should come on for that outing but he might need another run or two before he is of renewed interest.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner in France as a 2yo; low-key stable debut this month; may need more time

runners.name: Wild Nature
runners.spotlight: His best efforts for Godolphin came on AW; no show in two turf runs for Ed Bethell in the autumn but he bounced back to form wih a bang when making all in a 7f handicap at Kempton in November; a 2lb rise is manageable but there are other front-runners on show and he has a 197-day absence to overcome back on turf.
runners.comment: His three top RPRs have coincided with his three AW runs; other pace to deal with here

runners.name: Percy's Lad
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; well handicapped, being 8lb lower than for his Chester win last May, but he didn't fire at that meeting two weeks ago and he now has a point to prove.
runners.comment: On a good mark but not shone this year, including at Chester last time; other pace in here

races.race_name: Racing TV Profits Returned To Racing Handicap

runners.name: Alpine Sierra
runners.spotlight: 2.25l behind Spirit Catcher here last month (1m1f, good) and he is 2lb worse off with that rival today; did win well at Hamilton (1m3f, good) 18 days ago, however, and likely to be in the thick of it again.
runners.comment: Won well at Hamilton 18 days ago; behind Spirit Catcher last month and 2lb worse off today

runners.name: Cougar
runners.spotlight: Ended his 2025 campaign with a pair of 1m4f wins, the first of them here and by a wide margin; two heavy defeats this spring but he's the type to pop up soon, perhaps when faced with some slower ground.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two 1m4f wins; struggled in two runs this year but the type to pop up soon

runners.name: Star Cast
runners.spotlight: Having a fine year, winning for the fourth time when scoring at Ripon 13 days ago (1m2f, good); still feasibly weighted but previous 1m4f efforts haven't seen her convince with her stamina.
runners.comment: Having a good year and chasing a quick hat-trick; stamina for 1m4f still to be proven

runners.name: Spirit Catcher
runners.spotlight: Having tumbled down the weights he returned to form in a 1m1f handicap here last month, winning decisively; second over 1m2f at Ripon on Sunday, staying on at the finish; up in trip but likely to go well.
runners.comment: Arrives in form, winning over 1m1f here before a good 2nd at Ripon; new trip; key player

runners.name: Just Dottie
runners.spotlight: Four wins over hurdles; had few chances on the Flat but she won at Ayr in September (1m5f, soft) and showed better form when third over 1m7f 11 days later; still has some potential in this sphere and a hurdle run last month should have sharpened her up; might need more of a stamina test than this though.
runners.comment: Unexposed on the Flat but she may need more of a stamina test than this

runners.name: Spit Spot
runners.spotlight: Won twice on AW in her youth; three hurdle wins since joining this stable, the latest at Perth last October; not so good this spring and a wind op last month needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Three hurdle wins since last seen on the Flat; quiet on last two runs; had wind op

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Classy Clarets
runners.spotlight: Running well for Iain Jardine since February, including wins at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in March and Hamilton (5f, good) last week; unpenalised for that latest success, so 3lb well in, as he was when creditable third at Ayr (favourite) yesterday; should go well again if that hasn't taken it out of him.
runners.comment: In good form in recent months, winning on AW and at Hamilton; ran creditably yesterday

runners.name: Canaria Queen
runners.spotlight: Off the mark for the year with a determined effort in a Class 6 at Doncaster last month (5f, good to firm); not in the same form at Ayr 16 days ago but this C&D winner could rebound with a big run.
runners.comment: C&D winner; game effort at Doncaster last month but not in the same form at Ayr latest

runners.name: Fear And Fast
runners.spotlight: 17-race maiden; placed over C&D last October; low-key return to action at Ayr 16 days ago and he needs to leave that well behind.
runners.comment: 0-17; edging down the weights but he needs to leave his reappearance well behind him
runners.owner: Let's Be Lucky Racing 38 & Steven Wilson

runners.name: Wrestling Revenue
runners.spotlight: Well supported when winning a 5f handicap at Pontefract last October (soft ground); not matched that form since but he is back to the same mark now and returning to Class 6 company will help; trainer also saddles Sir Benedict; check the market.
runners.comment: Back on last winning mark and dropping to Class 6 can help; yard runs two; check market

runners.name: Sir Benedict
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner; style of racing leaves him a hostage to fortune and he has met trouble in two of his three runs for Ben Haslam; has the ability to win at this level and stall 1, while not optimal, should at least keep him clear of some of the traffic.
runners.comment: Capable at this level but his style of racing leaves him a hostage to fortune

runners.name: Invincible Crown
runners.spotlight: He has done his winning on AW and, although he handles turf, it has been a while since he ran to form on the grass; drops in class today but others look safer.
runners.comment: Seems happier on AW now; drops in class today but others look safer
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing VIII & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Royal Duke
runners.spotlight: Two of his three wins have come over C&D and he is 2lb lower than for the latest of them; unplaced on his recent return from a six-month absence; can take a step forward today but others are still more appealing for win purposes.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins on his CV; sharper for his recent reappearance; others are still preferred

runners.name: Soul Seeker
runners.spotlight: Regressive 9yo; beaten around 4l on his last two starts and needs to up his game to trouble the principals.
runners.comment: Regressive 9yo; needs to up his game if he is to trouble the principals

runners.name: Ganesha
runners.spotlight: Latest 6f run not the one to judge him on and his earlier 5f runs this year were both encouraging enough; on a dangerous mark if the new headgear has the desired effect.
runners.comment: Last week's 6f run can be excused; dangerous mark and new headgear could help

races.race_name: ICL Handicap

runners.name: Ebony Maw
runners.spotlight: 9yo who went 0-11 last year and was soundly beaten on last month's reappearance at Catterick; however, he may have needed that latest run and is on a competitive mark judged on last season's placed form.
runners.comment: 9yo whose losing run continues but he could improve for last month's comeback

runners.name: Dreams Adozen
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent for new yard in the second half of last year but posted wins at Musselburgh (2m2f, good to soft) and Nottingham (1m6f, heavy; when last seen); in the mix if at the top of her game on this reappearance.
runners.comment: Two wins for new yard last autumn; in the mix if at the top of her game on reappearance

runners.name: Treasure Islands
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who improved for the step up in trip when making all at Ripon (2m, good) last month on his second handicap start; can make another bold bid if transferring that latest promise to today's slower ground; his classy dam was right at home on soft going.
runners.comment: Improved for the step up to 2m when making all at Ripon on his second handicap start

runners.name: Fleurman
runners.spotlight: Won at Kempton (2m, AW) in March and solid third there (2m, AW) last month; rarely seen on turf nowadays but he was effective on grass early in his career (particularly on slow ground); visor replaces cheekpieces; respected.
runners.comment: In good form on Kempton on last two starts; effective on turf early in career; respected

runners.name: Goin'
runners.spotlight: Two-time German winner who is 0-11 for his current trainer and the forecast soft ground may not be ideal; however, after being denied a clear run he kept on well for seventh of 20 at Navan (1m5f, good to yielding) last month, beaten about 5l, and this Irish raider could have a big say back up in trip.
runners.comment: Kept on well for 7th of 20 over 1m5f at Navan and could have a big say back up in trip

runners.name: Torcello
runners.spotlight: His mark continues to tumble and he's one to watch in the betting in reapplied cheekpieces with the slow ground in his favour; however, this 12yo has very much struggled on his four starts since last June.
runners.comment: His mark continues to tumble but this 12yo has very much struggled over the last 12 months

races.race_name: Pitch Perfect EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Scarlet Legend
runners.spotlight: Brother to Goodwood Cup winner Quickthorn; 10-1 for recent debut at Salisbury (1m4f, good to firm) where he was beaten a short head by the odds-on favourite; strong claims on this second start.
runners.comment: Short-headed by the odds-on favourite on recent debut at Salisbury and has strong claims

runners.name: Outback Legend
runners.spotlight: Promise on both starts, finishing sixth at Nottingham (1m2f, good) last month and third of five at Chester (10.3f, good) recently; today's extra yardage could be a plus and he might not be far away.
runners.comment: Promise on both starts and today's extra yardage could be a plus; might not be far away

runners.name: Yafreh
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and beaten just over 7l when fourth of six on last month's debut at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm; ran without a shoe); however, he made some late headway and his attractive pedigree suggests he could have lots more to offer when things click; steps up in trip and could have an each-way chance.
runners.comment: No threat on debut but attractive pedigree suggests he could have lots more to offer

runners.name: Spirit Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Inexperience evident when well-beaten sixth of eight at 16-1 on recent debut at Salisbury (1m4f, good to firm); in good hands to improve but he was a long way behind stablemate Scarlet Legend and needs to be an entirely different proposition this afternoon.
runners.comment: Inexperience evident when well beaten on debut; needs to be entirely different proposition

runners.name: Alkaios
runners.spotlight: 100-1 when keeping on for fifth of eight, beaten about 8l, on debut at Southwell (1m3f, AW) last month; her dam was useful and she's open to improvement, but she has just over 6l to find with Moonrunner on the Southwell form.
runners.comment: Kept on for 5th at 100-1 at Southwell on last month's debut, but has plenty of work to do

runners.name: Brutus Maximus
runners.spotlight: 4,000gns 3yo; sixth foal; closely related to Japanese jumps winner, half-brother to two winners including Easily Amused (1m/1m3f; RPR 87); dam 1m AW winner (98); wears tongue-tie on belated debut; likely best watched.
runners.comment: 4,000gns 3yo; wears tongue-tie on belated debut; likely best watched

runners.name: Moonrunner
runners.spotlight: Built upon last December's debut fourth at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) when third at Southwell (1m3f, AW) three weeks ago, beaten just over 1l; he can give another good account and is open to further improvement, but others have more substance to their form.
runners.comment: Third at Southwell three weeks ago on second start but others have more substance to form

runners.name: Florida Dreams
runners.spotlight: Grade 2 bumper winner at Aintree in April 2023; well beaten over hurdles on last two starts (stable debut latest) but he's shown some useful form in that code and is an interesting contender on this Flat debut.
runners.comment: Grade 2 bumper winner and useful over hurdles; interesting contender on this Flat debut

runners.name: Dartmouth Jet
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground bumper at Worcester on debut last September; however, down the field at big odds at Southwell (1m3f/1m4f, AW) on his first two Flat starts and a transformation is needed.
runners.comment: Bumper winner but down the field at big odds on first two Flat starts

races.race_name: Unsung Heroes Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Borjina
runners.spotlight: Improved upon last September's debut fourth at Newcastle (7f, AW) to win by 2l when odds-on at Catterick (7f, good) four weeks ago; she's in good hands to continue to progress and has strong claims under a 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: Won at Catterick four weeks ago on second start and in good hands to continue to progress
runners.owner: All About York And Whatton Manor Stud

runners.name: Secret Of Life
runners.spotlight: 11-2 when winning with something to spare on debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last August; absent since but her dam was a 1m Listed/1m2f Group 3 winner and she could have lots more to offer for her top yard; leading claims under 7lb penalty.
runners.comment: Won on debut last August; likeable prospect and has leading claims on first start since

runners.name: Shushu
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m2f winner (RPR 90), closely related to winners Traisha (1m6f Listed) and Baby Pink (1m4f Listed), half-sister to smart 1m-1m3f winner Christophermarlowe, out of US 1m4f Grade 3 winner; from respected Newmarket stable and bred to have a bright future; she's one to consider on debut.
runners.comment: From respected Newmarket yard & bred to have bright future; she's one to consider on debut

runners.name: Eveninginparis
runners.spotlight: 26,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Messerschmitt (1m 2yo, RPR 90; useful 2m hurdle) and Goose Rock (5f-1m2f; 67); dam 7f/7.4f 2yo winner (88); useful standard needed on debut; the betting can guide.
runners.comment: 26,000gns yearling; half-sister to two winners; the betting can guide on debut

runners.name: Pointe Bleu
runners.spotlight: Beaten 12l when fourth of five at 5-2 on last month's debut at Newmarket (7f, good); however, she has an attractive pedigree and is with a leading stable, and may well be able to leave that initial form well behind.
runners.comment: Underwhelming on debut at Newmarket but may be able to leave that form well behind

runners.name: Mass For Kathleen
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to 7f/1m AW winner English Lady (RPR 63); dam unraced half-sister to some useful winners, out of 1m4f Listed winner; a watching brief is advised on debut.
runners.comment: Fourth foal; half-sister to 7f/1m AW winner (RPR 63); watching brief advised on debut

runners.name: Prima Bailarina
runners.spotlight: Encouraging efforts last autumn when fourth on her first two starts (7f, good to soft/AW); improvement is needed today but she's from a family connections have done extremely well with and it will be no surprise if she takes a step forward.
runners.comment: 4th on first two starts; from a family connections have done very well with; could improve

races.race_name: Team Effort Handicap

runners.name: Lion Of Mali
runners.spotlight: Progressive sort who made it three wins in four starts this year when squeezing through (hit on the nose by rival's whip) to lead towards the finish and beat the reopposing Eduardo Calderon at Leicester (7f, good to firm) 26 days ago; nudged up only 2lb and another bold bid looks assured if he as effective on softer ground (well held on good to soft last year but it was in a valuable sales race).
runners.comment: Edged out Eduardo Calderon at Leicester latest, making it three wins in last four starts

runners.name: Supreme Clarets
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but has shown he can be competitive from his mark, including fourth of 11 at Wetherby (7f, good to firm; acts on heavy) 25 days ago.
runners.comment: Now 0-8 but latest Wetherby fourth was creditable and all ground seems to come alike

runners.name: Eduardo Calderon
runners.spotlight: Good start to his handicap career when close second of 12 to Lion Of Mali on last month's 7f Leicester reappearance (good to firm); if he comes on for that run it's possible he can turns the tables on a rival who had race fitness on his side on that occasion; well held on good to soft last year but it's much too soon to suggest he can't cope with softer than good.
runners.comment: Promising second to Lion Of Mali on 7f Leicester handicap/seasonal debut; top of list

runners.name: Stoic Poet
runners.spotlight: Ended last year by finishing well held on his C&D handicap debut; slightly more encouraging signs when 4l fourth of eight on 6f Newcastle reappearance three weeks ago but he'll need to build on that, stepping back up to 7f.
runners.comment: Fair fourth on 6f Newcastle reappearance but needs to build on it now back at 7f

runners.name: Go Victor
runners.spotlight: Scored at Thirsk (7f, good) last June but he ended 2025 well out of sorts and there was no obvious sign of an upturn in his fortunes when down the field on AW reappearance six weeks ago.
runners.comment: Thirsk win last June but out of sorts since, including reappearance

runners.name: Euphonia
runners.spotlight: Improved when comfortable 3l winner of a 17-runner Leicester handicap (7f, good) 38 days ago, which was her first turf start since finishing fourth on debut last summer (also good); the Leicester second bombed out next time but bounced back to score at Redcar on Monday; raised 8lb but could easily still have some scope in her mark.
runners.comment: Raised 8lb for Leicester win but did it well and is unexposed at 7f and on turf

runners.name: Sparkling Pink
runners.spotlight: Won 6f Pontefract nursery (good) off 2lb higher last summer for Jonathan Portman; struggled over that trip on next four outings but took a step back in the right direction when third of 12 over 7f at Doncaster (good to firm) 26 days ago; the fourth and fifth from that race have run well to be placed since, so the form could be okay; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Back to form when third over 7f at Doncaster latest; enters calculations

runners.name: Sun Of Dolly
runners.spotlight: Raced around 6f so far, showing his best form when fourth of 13 on handicap debut at Windsor (good to firm) last time, faring best of those held up; 7f is likely to be within range on that evidence; not discounted.
runners.comment: Nearest finish when fourth on 6f Windsor handicap debut, so 7f could suit

runners.name: Kiani King
runners.spotlight: All three starts were 6f on AW; showed promise when fourth at Wolverhampton on first two; hung badly left on bend at Kempton since, so it's no surprise to see him return to a left-handed course for this handicap and turf debut over a longer trip.
runners.comment: Promise first two runs; hung left at Kempton latest; back left-handed for handicap debut
runners.owner: P C J Dalby & R D Schuster

runners.name: Tekitoff
runners.spotlight: Has made a positive start for his new yard, building on promising handicap debut third when winning over 7f at Doncaster (good to firm) 19 days ago; only 2lb higher but the subsequent exploits of horses from that race don't suggest it's particularly strong form.
runners.comment: Won at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) latest but the form has taken a few knocks
runners.owner: Ian Barker, Matthew Hunt & Sarah Turner

runners.name: This Rib
runners.spotlight: Not devoid of ability on his first two starts (7f, AW) but well held on turf on final outing; has been gelded ahead of this handicap debut after seven months off; the betting should help guide to expectations.
runners.comment: Showed ability on two of three starts last year; gelded ahead of this handicap debut

runners.name: Bluebluelectricblu
runners.spotlight: Not obviously well handicapped on the form she showed in four maiden/novice events over 6f/7f last year, so will need to return from a five-month break with an improved effort.
runners.comment: Not obviously well treated on the form she showed in maiden/novice events last year

races.race_name: No Sport Without A Surface Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Regal Tiger
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns foal, £50,000 yearling; fifth foal; Kingman half-brother to winners Sea Baaeed (7f AW 2yo; RPR 95) and Mohatu (1m AW; 86); dam 7f AW winner (99) from classy family; trainer has made an excellent start with his 2yos and he's one to consider on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer has made an excellent start with his 2yos and he's one to consider on debut

runners.name: Keep Kicking On
runners.spotlight: 20-1 when always in rear on recent debut at Ascot (6f, good); takes on some very interesting newcomers today but he's the only one with experience and it will be no surprise if he takes a sizeable step forward.
runners.comment: Always in rear on recent debut at Ascot but no surprise if he takes sizeable step forward

runners.name: Charted Course
runners.spotlight: 100,000gns yearling, 450,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; by Palace Pier; seventh foal; closely related to Listed-placed 7f winner Fakhama (RPR 97), half-brother to winners Mujtaba (Group-placed 7.5f-1m2f; 118) and Mawood (1m2f; 89); dam dual South African 1m Grade 1 winner (104); stable had a well-backed 2yo newcomer win at Newcastle on Tuesday; high on the list on debut.
runners.comment: Cost 450,000gns at last month's breeze-ups and he's high on the list on debut

runners.name: Le Ciel Noble
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros foal, £43,000 yearling; third foal; Supremacy half-brother to Italian 1m 2yo winner Fast Luxury; dam unraced half-sister to two winners; has been gelded; one of two newcomers for the yard and a market check is advised, but others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: One of two newcomers for yard and market check is advised, but others make greater appeal

runners.name: Persian Land
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros foal, £32,000 yearling; seventh foal; Persian Force half-brother to winners Thunderey (Scandinavian 6f-1m including 2yo), Donn Clifs (French/German/Czech 7.5f/1m) and Paparemobeach (Italian 7f/1m); gelded; trainer saddles two debutants; check the betting, but he may be best watched.
runners.comment: Trainer saddles two debutants; check the betting, but he may be best watched

runners.name: Walt
runners.spotlight: 19,000euros yearling, resold 40,000euros yearling, 82,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; seventh foal; Persian Force half-brother to four winners including Ingleby Ivy (6f 2yo; RPR 78); dam 6f 2yo/7f winner (81); could go well on debut.
runners.comment: 82,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; half-brother to four winners; could go well on debut

races.race_name: Early Bird Handicap

runners.name: Showtown
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-6 when disappointing favourite in first-time cheekpieces (retained) on recent handicap debut at Beverley (5f, good to soft); it's hard to be confident on the back of that but he was runner-up three times previously and is effective on soft ground, so it's too soon to write him off.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite on h'cap debut but promise previously; too soon to write him off

runners.name: Stoneacre Joe
runners.spotlight: All three starts over 6f on fast ground; left sole 2yo run well behind when runner-up on both outings this spring, most recently at Ayr where he was the only one to give the odds-on winner a race; his dam was useful (best form on soft going) and he has potential off his opening mark.
runners.comment: Runner-up on both starts this spring and he has potential off his opening mark

runners.name: Daizen
runners.spotlight: Down the field in good-ground handicaps on his two starts this year, the latest at Hamilton on Sunday; however, he won a soft-ground maiden at Ayr (6f) last September and the return to a slow surface may be just what he needs.
runners.comment: Hasn't shone this spring but on good ground; last year's maiden win came on soft going

runners.name: Angels' Share
runners.spotlight: Plenty in hand when winning two in a row at Lingfield last August/September (7f, AW; maiden/handicap); however, well beaten back on turf on both runs since and she has something to prove on grass.
runners.comment: Two commanding AW wins last August/September but twice well beaten on turf since

runners.name: Guernsey Angel
runners.spotlight: Two 5f AW nursery wins in the final third of last year; however, she's been unplaced over 5f on her four starts this year and the return to 6f doesn't appear to be an obvious plus.
runners.comment: Two-time 5f AW nursery winner; hasn't progressed and stamina to prove back up to 6f

runners.name: There's A Chance
runners.spotlight: Won on nursery debut at Hamilton (6f, good) last July; yet to kick on from that but her reappearance third at Newcastle (6f, AW) three weeks ago gives her something to build on and she's not ruled out.
runners.comment: Yet to kick on from last July's nursery debut win but she's not ruled out

runners.name: Hvar
runners.spotlight: 6f AW novice winner last October for Ralph Beckett; a market check is advised but he's been beaten a long way in handicaps this spring on his first two starts for this yard.
runners.comment: 6f AW 2yo novice winner but well beaten in handicaps on his two starts this spring

runners.name: Harlequin Bay
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two maidens at Newcastle (5f/6f, AW) towards end of last year; below his best at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in February when last seen but goes handicapping with his yard in good form and could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two AW maidens; goes handicapping with his yard in good form

runners.name: From The Hip
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt in a maiden at Chelmsford (6f, AW) last October and followed up in Kempton nursery (6f, AW); showed promise on soft ground last September; possible player if fully tuned up for comeback.
runners.comment: 2yo campaign concluded with two wins; possible player if fully tuned up for comeback

runners.name: Magistery
runners.spotlight: 2yo campaign ended with heavy defeats in a nursery and Listed race; however, she won a soft-ground 6f nursery at Carlisle last September and could be a contender if getting her career back on track following a break.
runners.comment: Tame end to 2yo campaign but won soft-ground 6f nursery at Carlisle last September

runners.name: Woolisle
runners.spotlight: Progressive during last year's eight-race campaign, finishing second in a nursery at Nottingham (6f, heavy) last October and going one better there (5f, soft) six days later; reappears with conditions in her favour; chance.
runners.comment: In good form in soft/heavy-ground nurseries last October; conditions to suit on return
runners.owner: Paul G Jacobs And Wetumpka Racing

races.race_name: First In, Last To Leave Handicap

runners.name: Brummell
runners.spotlight: Two wins last summer (5f, good/good to firm) and didn't see out 6f when third of six on recent reappearance at Doncaster (6f, good to firm); has run well on slow ground in the past and he's a key player back down to 5f.
runners.comment: Didn't see out 6f on his reappearance but ran well; key player back down to 5f

runners.name: Wheels Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Won at Musselburgh (5f, soft) last month and bounced back from lesser run at Newbury (6f, good) when sixth of ten at Thirsk (5f, good to firm), beaten just over 2l; back on slow ground today and could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Soft-ground winner at Musselburgh last month; ran okay last time; could be in the mix

runners.name: Accrual
runners.spotlight: On a handy mark on his best AW form last winter and he's also effective on turf; however, he hasn't shone since being short-headed at Lingfield in January and needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: On a handy mark on best form but hasn't shone since being short-headed in January

runners.name: Trilby
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when second at Beverley (5f, good) last month; sixth of seven there since (5f, good to soft) but beaten less than 2l and he's a three-time winner here (6f/5f, good-heavy); could be bang there now back here in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Beverley two starts ago and he's a 3-time winner here; could be bang there

runners.name: Isle Of Lismore
runners.spotlight: 8yo who is running for the first time since last July and his losing sequence goes back ten races to May 2024; however, he went close when last seen and he's right at home on slow ground; capable of having a say.
runners.comment: Returns from an absence but on a competitive mark and he's right at home on slow ground

runners.name: The Bell Conductor
runners.spotlight: His mark continues to fall but for good reason and this 9yo arrives with plenty to prove; wears a first-time tongue-tie; visor is removed.
runners.comment: His mark continues to fall but for good reason and this 9yo arrives with plenty to prove

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: The Tunguska Event
runners.spotlight: A dual 6f winner at Newcastle over the winter and back from eight weeks off with a very good second of 14 in 6f Pontefract handicap last month; up 2lb but not out of things.
runners.comment: Very good second at Pontefract last month; up 2lb but not out of things

runners.name: Taaklam
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise at 2yrs for Ed Walker and has built on it for his current handler, hooded when runner-up in 6f Southwell handicap three weeks ago; a likely player in his bid for a breakthrough success despite a 2lb rise.
runners.comment: Good runner-up in 6f Southwell h'cap three weeks ago; a likely player despite a 2lb rise

runners.name: Yachtsman
runners.spotlight: Confirmed earlier promise for his current yard to gain a first win in 6f Windsor handicap three days ago; scored readily there so big shout under a penalty with talented claimer Harry Vigors in the saddle.
runners.comment: First win at Windsor three days ago; scored readily there so big shout under a 6lb penalty

runners.name: Hallandale Beach
runners.spotlight: Has been brought along steadily, fifth of eight in 5f Doncaster novice 26 days ago; tongue tied for his first go in handicaps back up in trip and capable of better.
runners.comment: Has been brought along steadily; tongue tied for h'cap debut back up in trip; more to come

runners.name: Lanarra
runners.spotlight: Much improved on her AW debut when a 66-1 winner of 6f nursery at Newcastle in October; she needs to back it up now returned to turf after seven months off the track.
runners.comment: 66-1 winner at Newcastle in October; needs to back it up now returned to turf

races.race_name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Got The Booty
runners.spotlight: Twice a runner-up in 6f AW novices before posting a solid fourth of nine in 7f Southwell maiden last month, leading long way; not out of things reverted in trip here.
runners.comment: Solid fourth at Southwell last month, leading long way; not out of things reverted in trip

runners.name: Rouane
runners.spotlight: Hooded and took a big step forward from her debut third when beaten a neck in 6f Newbury novice in August; off since but yard continues to go really well so this daughter of Oasis Dream holds leading claims.
runners.comment: Beaten neck in Newbury novice in August; yard going really well so holds leading claims

runners.name: Why Because
runners.spotlight: Showed some fair form over 1m in a light campaign for Harry Charlton in 2025; last of six in 6f Redcar novice on her yard debut last month so needs to take a big step forward in a refitted hood.
runners.comment: Fair form for Harry Charlton; last at Redcar on yard debut last month though

runners.name: Moonlight Melody
runners.spotlight: Mohaather filly; dam, 1m6f/2m AW winner (RPR 78), is a sister to 1m4f AW winner Point Of View and half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Magic Artist; the market can guide on her debut.
runners.comment: Mohaather filly; the market can guide on her debut

runners.name: Lady Gormire
runners.spotlight: Promising debut second in 6f Haydock maiden last July but only eighth of ten in 7f Doncaster maiden two months later on her final run for Kevin Ryan; has since changed hands for 12,000gns and not written off.
runners.comment: Failed to build on debut promise when eighth at Doncaster for Kevin Ryan; not written off

runners.name: Dolly Golightly
runners.spotlight: 9-1, always behind when eighth of ten in novice at Redcar (5f, good) on her debut last April; she needs to hit the ground running after her absence.
runners.comment: Eighth at Redcar on her debut last April; needs to hit ground running after her absence

races.race_name: catterickbridge.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Crocodile Power
runners.spotlight: Four C&D wins (mostly on soft), the latest when seeing off Chief Of State last October; has twice finished behind Evocative Spark over 7f here this year, so has work to do with that reopposing rival; latest effort - runner-up - was a definite step back in the right direction.
runners.comment: Four C&D wins (mostly on soft); has twice finished behind Evocative Spark here this year.

runners.name: Moostar
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 14 starts came at Thirsk (6f nursery; good to soft) in September 2024; she has been in decent form this year though, runner-up to Keldeo over C&D (Class 5 fillies') in April and pipped on the post in an added visor at Wolverhampton (6f; 13-8 favourite) latest; 2lb higher.
runners.comment: 1-14; pipped on the post in an added visor at Wolverhampton (6f; 13-8 favourite) latest.

runners.name: Safari Dream
runners.spotlight: 7-43; took full advantage of an easing mark to gain his third Salisbury win when making all (6f apprentices', good) last week; escapes a penalty, and Jake Dickson is able to claim his 7lb allowance, so his chance is an obvious one; clearly the one to beat if in the same kind of form.
runners.comment: Made all at Salisbury (6f, good) last week; escapes a penalty; the one to beat.

runners.name: Evocative Spark
runners.spotlight: Thrives here - form figures 72111 - twice accounting for Vince Le Prince over 7f last month; weakened at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) 19 days ago, but this track is a much better fit, and he's unlikely to be hindered by rain; still fairly treated off 67 based on historic form.
runners.comment: Won on last three visits to Catterick; still fairly treated off 67 based on historic form.

runners.name: Ingleby Archie
runners.spotlight: 4-25; won from 3lb higher at York (6f, good) last May, and also went close (runner-up) back there in July; pestered for the lead, weakening late, when fourth on seasonal reappearance at Wetherby (5.5f; 15-8 favourite; form franked); now 2lb lower and with Jack Nicholls back aboard; chance.
runners.comment: Fourth on seasonal reappearance at Wetherby (5.5f; 15-8 fav); now sits 2lb lower; chance.

runners.name: Fiscal Policy
runners.spotlight: Done the bulk of his winning on AW (6-26), but one of his two turf wins came over C&D (from 8lb lower) in August 2024; in fine fettle, with form figures of 322, since returning to turf, beaten just over 2l by Mumayaz at Leicester (6f, good to firm) latest; should give his best.
runners.comment: In fine fettle, with form figures of 322, since returning to turf; should give his best.
runners.owner: S Thompson, Mrs R Carr & G Shields

runners.name: Beyond Borders
runners.spotlight: 5-24; hit by the handicapper after making all at Yarmouth (6f, good) last June, and has taken a while to come back down the weights (now 3lb below that winning mark); posted low-key seasonal reappearance at Leicester; cheekpieces introduced seeking more.
runners.comment: Posted low-key seasonal reappearance at Leicester; cheekpieces introduced seeking more.

runners.name: Willolarupi
runners.spotlight: 3-28, his most recent win from a similar mark at Redcar (6f, good to firm) last May; ended last season with a creditable third to City Captain at Pontefract (6f, soft), so won't wind whatever the weather does; bit to take on trust off a 227-day absence.
runners.comment: Ended last season with creditable third at Pontefract (6f, soft); makes reappearance.

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Nightsinwhitesatin
runners.spotlight: Largely progressive sort in 2025 (when a two-time 1m2f winner) and she's continued on the up stepped up to 1m4f this season, not enjoying the smoothest of passages when fourth of 15 at Chester 13 days ago; a player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Not smoothest of passages when fourth at Chester 13 days ago; player off an unchanged mark
runners.owner: David Kilburn, Mrs Corbett, Chris Wright

runners.name: Analogical
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways at Newcastle (1m4f) in April and backed it up with a solid third of five in 1m6f Thirsk handicap 19 days ago; in the mix once more.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle and backed it up with solid Thirsk third 19 days ago; in the mix again

runners.name: Made All
runners.spotlight: Scored at Wolverhampton (1m4f) in March and comes here on the back of a good hurdling third at Market Rasen 13 days ago; this C&D winner must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Good hurdling third at Market Rasen 13 days ago; this C&D winner must enter calculations

runners.name: Percy's Daydream
runners.spotlight: Made light of a seven-month absence when making all in 1m2f Doncaster handicap 27 days ago; up 5lb but she's not taken lightly with this return to further a likely plus.
runners.comment: Made all in 1m2f Doncaster h'cap 27 days ago; up 5lb but not taken lightly back up in trip

runners.name: Ludo's Landing
runners.spotlight: Refitted with blinkers when scoring at Newcastle (1m4f) in March but well below par both runs since, visored when a remote fourth of five there (1m4f) 20 days ago; blinkers go back on now.
runners.comment: Back in blinkers when scoring at Newcastle in March but well below par both runs since

races.race_name: Free Digital Racecard At raceday-ready.com Handicap

runners.name: Musical Soldier
runners.spotlight: Raised his game and off the mark, making all, at Wolverhampton (7f; 17-2) just over a fortnight ago; now 5lb higher; every chance if proving as effective back on turf.
runners.comment: Made all at Wolverhampton (7f) 16 days ago; every chance back on turf from 5lb higher.

runners.name: Hood Wink
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated at Wetherby (1m, good to firm; 12-1) on penultimate start; paid the price for a rapid mid-race move at Ripon (fourth of seven, beaten over 5l) subsequently; isn't without a chance from 4lb above his mark at Wetherby; stablemate of Lope Y Linda.
runners.comment: Dead-heated at Wetherby (1m) penultimate; rapid mid-race move didn't work at Ripon latest.

runners.name: South Coast Star
runners.spotlight: Campaigned exclusively on AW (0-7); runner-up at Wolverhampton (7f; 12-1) on penultimate start and a repeat of that would give her a realistic chance; latest Kempton effort (beaten 11l) was less encouraging but this sharp track could suit her forward-going run style.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Wolverhampton (7f) penultimate; this sharp track could suit her run style.

runners.name: Lope Y Linda
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden; gave a decent account until fading when fourth to the progressive Mohmentous at Redcar (1m, good; 9-1) a fortnight ago; dropping back to an easy 7f should suit so this stablemate of Hood Wink can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Faded when fourth to the progressive Mohmentous at Redcar (1m) a fortnight ago; possible.

runners.name: Trucial Pearl
runners.spotlight: Yet to win (0-10) but just failed to do so in a newly applied visor at Chelmsford (7f; 12-1) in March; fairly respectable since, a weakening fifth at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) latest; Mason Paetel's 5lb claim might assist from a handicapping perspective.
runners.comment: 0-10; runner-up in added visor at Chelmsford (7f) in March; fairly respectable since.

runners.name: Dream Forever
runners.spotlight: Maiden (0-9); runner-up on AW at Newcastle (6f; 18-1) in February; beaten 29l (13th of 14) and 8l (12th of 14) at Pontefract and Redcar respectively on his last two appearances; easing mark and claimer ridden but difficult to make a case for on recent form.
runners.comment: 0-9; easing mark and claimer ridden but difficult to make a case for on recent form.

runners.name: Hear The Drums
runners.spotlight: Nursery winner at Leicester (7f, soft; from 4lb lower) last October; gelded over the winter and failed to see out 1m (good to firm) on seasonal reappearance at Redcar; may strip fitter, forecast rain is helpful, and Ethan Tindall claims a handy 5lb.
runners.comment: Failed to see out 1m on seasonal reappearance at Redcar; Ethan Tindall claims handy 5lb.

runners.name: Belling The Cat
runners.spotlight: Runner-up to Liquid Cooled at Newcastle (6f; 14-1) in March and not far off those levels at Redcar (fourth) and Southwell (fifth) since; tackles 7f for the first time - isn't an obvious move on pedigree - in newly fitted cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Newcastle (6f) in March; tackles 7f for the first time in added cheekpieces.

runners.name: Antique Blue
runners.spotlight: Habitual slow starter for whom it is yet to click since joining Ruth Carr; soundly beaten at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; 28-1) just under a month ago; now 4lb lower - a career-low mark - seeking something positive; focus elsewhere.
runners.comment: Habitual slow starter for whom it is yet to click since joining R. Carr; focus elsewhere.
runners.owner: Miss K Padgett & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Supreme Dancer
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden; best effort came when headed close home at Musselburgh (1m, good to soft) last September; ended last season on a downbeat note (AW); gelded and best watched off a six-month break; stablemate of Hear The Drums.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden; ended last season on a downbeat note (AW); gelded and best watched.

runners.name: Rocklaw
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on turf debut at Thirsk (1m, good; fourth of six, 50-1) in early April, but took a significant backward step at Redcar (1m); plenty of questions to answer for the time being; stablemate of Trucial Pearl.
runners.comment: Took a significant backward step at Redcar (1m); plenty of questions to answer for now.

races.race_name: Bet At racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: Filey Beach
runners.spotlight: 3-23; did too much too soon at Musselburgh (7f; fourth, 6-4 favourite; third has won since) on penultimate start; made amends over C&D ten days ago, beating Crocodile Power (runs in the 3.10pm) by over 2l; that was an apprentices' handicap so he escapes a penalty (is due a 5lb rise); key player.
runners.comment: C&D apprentice winner ten days ago; escapes a penalty; key player in winnable contest.

runners.name: Emerald Army
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (for Declan Carroll) last July and he competes here from a 5lb lower mark; lacked the pace to challenge on first two starts for Ruth Carr (who also saddles Filey Beach) so stepping back up to 7f rates as a positive move; wide draw, yet still merits respect.
runners.comment: Lacked the pace to challenge on first two starts for R. Carr; return to 7f in his favour.

runners.name: East Tyrone
runners.spotlight: Doesn't find winning easy (2-28) but is consistent for the level; third in four of his last five AW starts, including when keeping on behind Mount Ruapehu at Southwell (6f; 14-1) 23 days ago; wide draw isn't ideal for turf return; stablemate of Indy's Angel.
runners.comment: 2-28; third in four of his last five AW starts; wide draw isn't ideal for turf return.
runners.owner: Alan And David Fairhurst And P Kirby

runners.name: Without Delay
runners.spotlight: Most effective at sharp tracks; justified favouritism when fending off Ideal Guest over C&D (good to firm) last July and now 5lb lower; step back in the right direction when third to Filey Beach over C&D (14-1, beaten over 2l) ten days ago; interesting to see if she can back that up.
runners.comment: Third to Filey Beach over C&D (14-1) ten days ago; interesting to see if she can back up.

runners.name: Dolly's Delight
runners.spotlight: Won for Julie Camacho at Beverley (7.5f; good to firm; from 3lb lower) last July; respectable across three starts for this trainer but does have some work to do with Saisons d'Or from Southwell (fifth, beaten 3l in an added tongue-tie); the task isn't impossible.
runners.comment: Some work to do with Saisons d'Or from Southwell (fifth); the task isn't impossible.
runners.owner: The Good Racing Company Ltd & Partner

runners.name: Indy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 18 starts came at Newcastle (6f nursery) in September 2024; hasn't offered much in three runs this year (6f), so cheekpieces are added seeking a basic spark; stablemate East Tyrone is a better option.
runners.comment: Hasn't offered much in three runs this year (6f); cheekpieces added seeking a basic spark.

runners.name: Saisons d'Or
runners.spotlight: Veteran; game AW winner at Southwell (7f; 6-1) recently; 1lb rise looks fair and he's still 5lb lower than when successful over C&D (good to firm) last August; no reason why he shouldn't be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Game AW winner at Southwell (7f) recently; 1lb rise fair and he's a three-time C&D winner.

runners.name: Blue Jay Way
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back, here (6f) and Wolverhampton (7f), last October; higher in the weights now and Ethan Tindall is booked for Ideal Guest; raced freely when seventh of nine at Wolverhampton (7f; 16-1) two months ago; others more obvious.
runners.comment: Won back-to-back, here (6f) & Wolverhampton (7f), last October; raced freely last time.

runners.name: Roundhay Park
runners.spotlight: 13-91; won from this mark at Ripon (6f, soft; Ideal Guest third) last July and was runner-up at Pontefract (from 4lb higher) last September; weakened - eighth of nine - on last month's C&D comeback run (66-1) but that's likely to have brought him on.
runners.comment: 13-91; won from this mark at Ripon (6f, soft) last July; fitter for C&D comeback run?

runners.name: Monotone
runners.spotlight: Juvenile winner for Jim Bolger (Cork, 5f maiden, heavy); disappointing since joining this trainer (0-6), falling 20lb in the weights; slowly away and never involved at Thirsk (6f, good; 40-1) latest; hard to recommend now returning to 7f.
runners.comment: Disappointing since joining this trainer (0-6), falling 20lb in weights; hard to recommend

runners.name: Ideal Guest
runners.spotlight: 5-40; runner-up to Without Delay over C&D (good to firm; in cheekpieces) last July and found another of these rivals - Roundhay Park - too strong at Ripon (in a visor) next time; ended last season on a downbeat note and no win since October 2023; monitor only.
runners.comment: 5-40; ended last season on a downbeat note and no win since October 2023; monitor only.

races.race_name: Racing Again 30th May Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Velvet Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Showed promise at two despite being headstrong; much more settled and took a big step forward to land 7f handicap at Newmarket 19 days ago; up 3lb but remains on a very workable mark; major player.
runners.comment: More settled when scoring at Newmarket on return; major player despite 3lb weights rise
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Ten Sixty Six
runners.spotlight: Placed on her first three runs in 7f maidens last August; beat only one rival after seven months off on her handicap debut at Nottingham (1m) last month but should strip sharper here.
runners.comment: Beat only one after 7 months off at Nottingham last month but should strip sharper here

runners.name: Blufferonthebus
runners.spotlight: A three-time 7f scorer in 2025; hinted at a revival when sixth of 13 in 8.5f handicap at Beverley nine days ago, finishing well from a poor draw; very much one to consider.
runners.comment: Hinted at revival with Beverley sixth latest, finishing well; very much one to consider

runners.name: Rosemary May
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in 1m Ayr maiden last April but she has failed to kick on in handicaps, last of ten on her return at Ripon (1m) 26 days ago; hood is reached for.
runners.comment: Last of ten on her return at Ripon (1m) 26 days ago; hood is reached for now

runners.name: Amelia's Joy
runners.spotlight: Made it two wins in her last three starts in 7f Wolverhampton handicap last month; up 5lb and needs to reproduce it on her first turf run since her debut.
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton latest; up 5lb and needs to reproduce it on only her second turf run

runners.name: Thehunnebelllegacy
runners.spotlight: Proved consistent without winning at two, though she went too keenly when fourth in 6f Newcastle nursery on her final run in November; she can't be ruled out if more settled in 2026.
runners.comment: Too keen when fourth at Newcastle final run; can't be ruled out if more settled in 2026
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Sahara Magic
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start in 6f Southwell maiden in November but she's been well below par since, beating just one on her handicap debut there (7f) in January; lots more needed on her first go on turf after a layoff.
runners.comment: Made a winning start in Southwell maiden in November but she's been well below par since

runners.name: Homestrait
runners.spotlight: A dual 7f winner in 2025; got back on track when fourth of eight in 6f handicap here ten days ago so not out of things with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Back on track when fourth here ten days ago; not out of things with cheekpieces added

races.race_name: Calor Gas Handicap Chase

runners.name: Pahlavi
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough success over hurdles came at Downpatrick last summer (2m2f, good); only second chase start on return at Wexford a fortnight ago when well beaten (well held in Roscommon handicap last summer).
runners.comment: Won over hurdles at Downpatrick last summer; two chase starts so far have been poor

runners.name: Prince Of Air
runners.spotlight: Hurdles and three-time chase winner; best form at Tramore; was demoted from first in Killarney chase last summer; never sighted in first three starts for this yard; blinkers go on now.
runners.comment: Tramore specialist; never sighted in first three starts for this yard; blinkers go on now

runners.name: Finnicky Filly
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles at Clonmel in December (heavy) and little of note until staying-on second over 2m3f at Kilbeggan last month (good to yielding); only a fair sixth at Clonmel last week; can be a tricky ride but top claimer aboard.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles on heavy at Clonmel in December; can be a tricky ride but good claimer on

runners.name: Donnrua Dream
runners.spotlight: Won here over hurdles in February (15.5f, heavy) and sent off favourite for 19.5f course handicap chase on latest when a never-threatening fourth; has a bit to find but the drop in trip should help.
runners.comment: Enjoys it around here, with a hurdles win in February; didn't stay last time; player

runners.name: Stily
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran some good races in defeat last year, including when runner-up at Thurles (18.5f, yielding) when last seen in October under a forceful ride; the blinkers worn that day are disgarded; 182-day absence.
runners.comment: Maiden hit the frame several times last year; blinkers worn last time are disgarded

runners.name: Ellies Rock
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at Clonmel last month when dropped to 2m (soft); stamina test remains in doubt after moderate fifth at Tramore (20.5f) on latest; half-sister to high-class chaser Thunder Rock so must be of interest on chase bow back down in trip.
runners.comment: Around 2m is her optimum; half-sister to high-class chaser Thunder Rock; chase debut

runners.name: Falcon Park
runners.spotlight: Best effort for this yard when second behind a well handicapped winner over hurdles at Clonmel in February; reverts to chasing now (won over fences for Emmet Mullins in the summer of 2024); others preferred.
runners.comment: Has ability judged on his Clonmel second over hurdles in February but hard to trust

runners.name: Mic Drop
runners.spotlight: Point/bumper winner; reverted to hurdling at Clonmel on latest after two moderate runs over fences but in rear throughout; others are more appealing at present.
runners.comment: Reverted to hurdling at Clonmel on latest after two moderate runs over fences but in rear

runners.name: L'Amiral Fromentin
runners.spotlight: First real effort of note on penultimate when decent third (beaten 3l) in Tramore novice handicap chase (15.5f, yielding) last month; fair sixth in better race at Wexford a fortnight ago; 5yo has scope to keep improving.
runners.comment: Good third at Tramore before sixth in better race at Wexford; 5yo can keep improving

runners.name: Party Pack
runners.spotlight: Maiden produced his best run when second in 2m1f Wexford handicap hurdle last August; not a bad comeback effort at Cork but tailed off six days later at Down Royal; chase debut.
runners.comment: Posted a couple of fair efforts over hurdles; tailed off at Down Royal; chase debut

runners.name: Big Island
runners.spotlight: Went close here off 2lb higher in April last year (19.5f, good); four tame efforts followed but there with every chance when falling two out at Tramore when last seen in August; contender if ready to roll after an absence.
runners.comment: Went close here in April 2025; fell two out at Tramore when with winning chance in August

runners.name: Quinn The Eskimo
runners.spotlight: Good ground hurdles winner shaped with mild promise on chase debut in Thurles beginners' (18.5f, good to yielding) in October; pulled up and tailed off next two starts on heavy; of interest if the ground has good in the description.
runners.comment: Hurdles winner on good; chase promise at Thurles in October; chance if ground is decent

runners.name: Brave Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 9yo; best effort in course handicap hurdle on latest when fourth of 13 (19.5f, soft to heavy; the third has won since); won a point so could make a chaser; market can guide.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 9yo; solid fourth here over hurdles on latest; former point winner

runners.name: Patrick Street
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; dual-purpose performer won here on the Flat last month; best work at the finish when third over hurdles at Punchestown last week; chase debut.
runners.comment: Dual-purpose performer won here on the Flat last month; chase debut; reserve

runners.name: Max Time
runners.spotlight: Galway win in September 2024 was a flash in the pan; unplaced in 33 of his 34 starts; pulled up in 3m handicap hurdle at Punchestown last week.
runners.comment: Galway win in September 2024 was a flash in the pan; unplaced in 33 of his 34 starts

runners.name: Getaway Henry
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; surprise hurdles winner at Wexford in 2024; best effort last season when runner-up at Thurles in October; chase debut.
runners.comment: Surprise winner at Wexford in 2024; makes chase debut after a break; reserve

runners.name: Spread The Faith
runners.spotlight: Maiden was pulled up in her last three hurdles outings; needs to leave all known form well behind on chase debut.
runners.comment: Pulled up in her last three outings; needs to leave all known form well behind; chase bow

runners.name: Benzine
runners.spotlight: Winless in five Flat starts and 13 over timber; hasn't looked as though the breakthrough is close, 30l behind at Thurles in October; chase debut.
runners.comment: Winless in five Flat starts and 13 over timber; makes chase debut after an absence

runners.name: Twoplustwo Equals
runners.spotlight: First reserve; maiden from 16 starts under rules; 1-6 in points of late; makes rules debut for this yard.
runners.comment: Maiden from 16 starts under rules; 1-6 in points of late; rules debut for yard; reserve

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares Chase

runners.name: Look To The West
runners.spotlight: 1-4 over fences and rated 135; she is the class horse in the field but she has fallen on her last two starts so she comes with risks attached; the one to beat if she puts in a clear round of jumping.
runners.comment: The class act of the field, has fallen the last twice, the one to beat with a clear round

runners.name: Broomfield Bijou
runners.spotlight: The most experienced mare in the field and rated 123 over fences; has often been highly tried to no avail, including when tailed off in Punchestown mares' Grade 2 latest; sights are lowered now and seems to handle most ground; chance.
runners.comment: Tailed off in Gr' 2 latest, sights lowered now, experience edge, ground versatile, chance

runners.name: Eye Of A Tiger
runners.spotlight: Rated 106 over hurdles; was beaten when falling in sole chase start in a Tramore novice (2m, soft); 8l fifth in handicap hurdle at Clonmel latest (16.5f, gd-yld); stamina in pedigree so could improve for this longer trip but others preferred.
runners.comment: 106-rated hurdler, fell sole chase start, could improve for this trip, others preferred

runners.name: Affinity Rock
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner made a very pleasing start to her chasing career at Killarney (2m5f, gd-yld) when a 0.5l second of 12 on return from 283 days off under a positive ride; obvious chance if building on that.
runners.comment: Fine run on chase debut after break on latest, obvious chance if building on that

runners.name: Justabitofyours
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over fences and badly out of sorts since running well in this race last year when beaten 1.75l by a 130-rated rival; big chance if repeating that but 2026 form figures of UPP do not inspire confidence.
runners.comment: Lost way since fine second in this race last year, hard to fancy on this year's form

races.race_name: Mann Family & Richard Egan Electrical Beginners Chase

runners.name: Le Coq Hardi
runners.spotlight: Both hurdle wins came on good ground at about 2m; yet to score in six starts over fences but rated 11lb higher in this sphere courtesy of some good runs in defeat, including when a 15l fourth in the Kerry National at Listowel in September (3m, yielding); tailed off when pulled up at Ascot (3m, good) when last seen in November; top-rated over fences and goes well fresh.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner and top-rated over fences, courtesy of good runs in defeat

runners.name: Ma Jacks Hill
runners.spotlight: Point winner won a Gowran maiden hurdle last autumn before a fine second in a Galway novice to a subsequent Grade 1 winner; hasn't gone on from that and just fair handicap runs the last twice; has work to do against some of these rivals on chase bow.
runners.comment: Point/m'den hurdle winner was just fair in h'cap hurdles last twice; work to do

runners.name: Ocastle Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Dual French hurdles winner is yet to score for this yard but has run well in defeat at times, including when a runner-up at Punchestown on chasing debut (2m3f, soft); returned from over a year off at Cork (2m1f, yielding) when a 23l fourth behind a better-fancied stablemate; Townend rides for the first time over fences, so respected.
runners.comment: Entitled to come on for first run back at Cork after over a year off and Townend rides

runners.name: Soir De Garde
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the top-class State Man made a winning hurdles debut at Tramore in April last year (2m1f, yielding); ran a decent race behind stablemate El Fabiolo at Leopardstown (2m3f, soft to heavy); fading in third when falling at the last at Cork and poor jumping took its toll when pulled up at Punchestown; goes chasing now, but Townend rides Ocastle Des Mottes.
runners.comment: Half-brother to State Man goes chasing, but Townend rides another

runners.name: Shadow Paddy
runners.spotlight: Has made rapid progress since winning a low-grade handicap at Tipperary in May last year; secured fourth hurdles win at Listed level here in October (2m5f, yielding); Grade 1 level proved a bridge too far at Aintree last month; top-rated of these over hurdles; definite chance.
runners.comment: Top-rated of these over hurdles after making rapid progress last season; definite chance

runners.name: Give Him A Chance
runners.spotlight: Added a third hurdles win at Naas in February (2m, heavy); slipped up early at Wexford next time; seventh of ten completed finishers for his chase debut at Kilbeggan last time (2m7f, good to yielding); a bit to find on hurdles ratings against these.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdles winner has a bit to find on ratings against these
runners.owner: John T O'Connor & Michael P Gleeson

runners.name: Mister Pink
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner for Willie Mullins; moderate in two runs over fences for new stable since returning from over a year off; others more likely.
runners.comment: Moderate in two starts over fences since returning from over a year off; others preferred

races.race_name: Moloney's Bus Hire & Martin's Coaches Handicap Chase

runners.name: Toon Town
runners.spotlight: Wexford handicap hurdle winner (2m1f, soft) for Brendan Walsh in 2024 off 87; beaten a short head in a five-runner contest at Catterick (2m, good to soft) on final start for Gavin Cromwell in December; creditable runs in defeat in both starts for new yard, distant third at Killarney last time; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Creditable runs recently inc' when third latest; not ruled out

runners.name: Doyen Magic
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles at Clonmel in January; solid runs in defeat in handicap chases at Clonmel (2m, heavy) and here (2m2f, heavy); effective on good to yielding when successful over fences at Kilbeggan last summer off 87 (2m4f); player.
runners.comment: Solid runs in defeat in chases last twice; handles better ground; player

runners.name: General Clermont
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase winner, including here (2m2f, good to yielding); went close off 1lb lower at Listowel in September (2m6f, good to yielding); lacks a recent run but goes well fresh; a drying track would enhance his chance.
runners.comment: Two-time chase winner goes well fresh; better ground enhances his chance

runners.name: Springboard
runners.spotlight: Goes chasing off the back of a handicap hurdle win here last month off 80 (2m, soft to heavy; fine on good), keeping on well to lead at the death; should make a chaser; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Goes chasing off back of h'cap hurdle win; interesting contender

runners.name: The Dara Man
runners.spotlight: Modest strike-rate of 3-38 over fences with last win coming at Kilbeggan in August last year; jumping awry when pulled up in his two most recent starts in December; suited by better ground but others preferred.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate over fences; jumping awry when PU last twice; others preferred

runners.name: Carlas Big Jim
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins arrived in 2024 on heavy ground; beaten 27l over fences at Clonmel (2m7f, soft) in April, but he was only 2l down when falling at the second last hurdle here (2m4f, soft to heavy) later that month; 3lb wrong.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner in 2024; only 2l down when falling at penultimate hurdle last time

runners.name: Temptationinmilan
runners.spotlight: More than three years since his last racecourse win; okay run in a Cork hunters' chase when a 22l fifth but tailed off at Punchestown last time; blinkers go on now but unlikely.
runners.comment: More than three years since last racecourse win; tailed off last time; unlikely

runners.name: Glitz And Glamour
runners.spotlight: Modest point-to-point form from three starts; narrow winner of a Sligo maiden hurdle in August and ran respectably on chasing debut at Killarney in October; unseen since tailed off here later that month; in the mix if at her best.
runners.comment: Modest point form; m'den hurdle winner ran creditably on chase debut; tailed off last time

runners.name: Back The West
runners.spotlight: 0-24 under rules; pulled up at Cork over hurdles on his return and now jumps fences for the first time since 2022; 12lb wrong; others preferred.
runners.comment: 0-24 under rules and PU at Cork over hurdles latest; others preferred

runners.name: One Way Traffic
runners.spotlight: Winless from 13 under rules and yet to threaten; tailed off over hurdles at Cork when last seen in November with cheekpieces added (now left off); others more likely.
runners.comment: 0-13 under rules and tailed off over hurdles when last seen in Nov'; others more likely

runners.name: Golden Temple
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner hasn't distinguished himself in three starts over fences; not a bad run on the Flat at Dundalk when last seen; makes handicap chase debut.
runners.comment: Dual hurdles winner hasn't distinguished himself in three chase runs; okay Flat run latest

races.race_name: Munster Drilling Beginners Chase

runners.name: American Jukebox
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 145-rated chaser American Mike; off the mark over hurdles when making virtually all for a runaway win in an ordinary 2m6f Tramore maiden last season (soft); safely held twice afterwards; dropped away tamely after racing prominently on chase debut at Wexford (2m7f, good to yielding) last time; improvement needed.
runners.comment: Dropped away tamely on chase debut two weeks ago; improvement needed

runners.name: Ad Caelum
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over hurdles is yet to score from 12 runs over fences; recent form is concerning, tailed off in a Down Royal handicap chase earlier this month and sixth of eight over hurdles at Kilbeggan latest.
runners.comment: Four-time hurdles winner is 0-12 over fences and her recent form is concerning

runners.name: Rusty Harkness
runners.spotlight: Definite promise in all four hurdle starts over a variety of trips and ground; beaten 10l at Clonmel on handicap debut last time off 113 (2m7f, yielding); O'Keeffe chooses Some Song from stable's two representatives.
runners.comment: Definite promise in all four hurdle runs but O'Keeffe rides Some Song

runners.name: Some Song
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; won his first and only point-to-point and followed up with a fortuitous maiden hurdle victory at Down Royal (2m7f, good to yielding) when the front two departed; pulled up at Thurles in December but his sixth of 20 at Fairyhouse last time was more promising; heads the shortlist now sent chasing.
runners.comment: Debut point and easy m'den hurdle winner; decent hurdles run latest; heads shortlist

runners.name: Sporting Glance
runners.spotlight: Debut point-to-point winner bagged back-to-back wins over hurdles in September in a Galway handicap (2m7f, good to yielding) and a Downpatrick novice contest (2m6f, yielding); fell at the first at Galway next time and tailed off at Cheltenham when last seen in October; top-rated of these over hurdles.
runners.comment: Point winner bagged back-to-back hurdle wins last season; player if ready

runners.name: Haveigotnewsforyou
runners.spotlight: Beat the now 150-rated hurdler Gaucher by a neck in a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle in 2024 (2m3f, good to yielding); sent chasing on return from a long absence at Gowran (2m4f, heavy) when tailed off and unseated at the seventh at Leopardstown last time (2m6f, yielding; 12-1); Cobden rides for the first time and market support would be interesting.
