runners.comment: Tailed off back from a long absence and UR latest but Cobden rides now; check market

runners.name: Game On
runners.spotlight: Best form on good ground; won a Downpatrick maiden hurdle without much fuss this time last year (2m6f, good); unseen since pulled up at Kilbeggan in June on chase debut after suffering an overreach; may need this first run back.
runners.comment: Best form on good inc' m'den hurdle win; may need this first run back

runners.name: Clody Diamond
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; point-to-point winner in 2024; best of four maiden hurdle runs was when a 2.25l runner-up at Wexford in April last year (2m5f, yielding); decent fourth at Down Royal on handicap debut last time (2m1f, soft); improvement now chasing not ruled out.
runners.comment: Point winner has run creditably in defeat over hurdles; makes chasing debut

runners.name: Rue Taylor
runners.spotlight: One of two for trainer; two-time winner over hurdles at Wexford but no sign of her best form last season; tailed off on chasing debut at Kilbeggan (2m7f, good) last month when jumping lacked fluency.
runners.comment: No sign of her best form last season and tailed off on chasing debut latest

races.race_name: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Chase (Div I)

runners.name: Zolpharine
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; 1-17 over hurdles and extended record to 0-22 over fences on latest at Tramore (2m), jumping mistakes proving costly but beaten only 4l; longer trip not an issue.
runners.comment: Jumping issues when beaten only 4l over 2m at Tramore latest; raise in trip not an issue

runners.name: Likable Chancer
runners.spotlight: Winless in 31 attempts hurdling; 3-35 over fences, with two of those at Tramore; flopped when well fancied for course handicap hurdle in February; fair fifth over fences at Tramore last month.
runners.comment: Record of 3-35 over fences; okay fifth at Tramore on latest but veteran may struggle

runners.name: Jukebox Johnny
runners.spotlight: Best run over hurdles when 10l fourth in Cork handicap (2m4f, soft to heavy) in December; disappointing effort at Down Royal last month; runner-up on sole point outing so could improve on his chase bow; new headgear; stable in form.
runners.comment: Runner-up on sole point outing so could improve on his chase bow; new headgear
runners.owner: Anthony N Durkan & B M Durkan

runners.name: Royal Time
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner at Naas in December 2024; good effort on his fourth chase start at Down Royal in January (2m5f, yielding to soft) when third of 11 off this mark; tried 3m2f on latest but unseated rider when backpedalling; new headgear.
runners.comment: Chance judged on his Down Royal third over similar trip off this mark; new headgear

runners.name: Ferdia
runners.spotlight: Has shown nothing in seven starts since returning from over three years off the track; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Has shown nothing in seven starts since returning from over three years off the track

runners.name: Another Ocana
runners.spotlight: Both career wins at Limerick; went close here over hurdles in February; solid third of 12 in Tramore handicap chase last month (21.5f, good to yielding); in the hunt for the places back at his favourite venue.
runners.comment: Good third at Tramore last month; in the hunt for the places back at his favourite venue

runners.name: Hakensack
runners.spotlight: Disappointed at Thurles on final run for James Hannon but definite player judged on his second and third placings over hurdles at Limerick/Cork in December; 0-6 in points; stable/chase debut.
runners.comment: Has some solid form over hurdles; 0-6 in points; first run for new yard; chase debut
runners.owner: Mrs J E Rothwell/Tipp Of Clare Syndicate/L Behan

runners.name: Reve Inoui
runners.spotlight: Point winner returned from a long absence with two poor hurdle runs; however, much better in two chase outings since and would be no surprise if he went close off a falling mark.
runners.comment: Much better last 2 chase outings and no surprise if he went close off a falling mark

runners.name: Doyen Wolf
runners.spotlight: Two best efforts over hurdles came off the back of a break at Sligo, eyecatcher there on latest when weaving through traffic to finish third of 16 (2m4f, good); runner-up to Cheltenham Festival winner Sine Nomine on sole point outing so should take to fences.
runners.comment: Two best efforts over hurdles at Sligo; runner-up to Cheltenham Festival winner sole point

runners.name: Tuff Days
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-31 under rules; best of three point runs of late when runner-up at Dromahane last month; talented claimer rode a winner for the yard last month so a market check is advised nonetheless.
runners.comment: Talented claimer rode a winner for the yard last month so a market check is advised

runners.name: Another Challenge
runners.spotlight: Took a while to find his feet but best effort yet when second of 15 at Down Royal's Christmas meeting (20.5f, good to yielding), keeping on well in closing stages; finished in front of Royal Time that day; big player after a break from 4lb higher.
runners.comment: Best effort yet when second of 15 at Down Royal's Christmas meeting; big player

runners.name: Ranger Billy
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner; badly out of form last summer; back from absence when pulled up in Cork handicap hurdle; can only be watched on second chase start (pulled up only other try).
runners.comment: Can only be watched on second chase start (pulled up only other try)

runners.name: Eddies Pride
runners.spotlight: Both chase wins have been over further and hasn't won in over two years; impossible task at weights in veterans' handicap at this course when last seen but still hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Both chase wins have been over further and hasn't won in over two years; others preferred

runners.name: Three Tribes
runners.spotlight: Never a factor in seven hurdle starts under rules; narrow winner of Ballysteen point-to-point last month; handicap chase debut.
runners.comment: Never a factor in 7 hurdle starts under rules; narrow winner of Ballysteen point latest

races.race_name: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Chase (Div II)

runners.name: Snow Punt
runners.spotlight: Point winner; 0-16 under rules but was beaten only a nose over C&D in March off 95 on heavy before going close at both Clonmel and Down Royal; has to be high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Beaten a nose over C&D before placed efforts at Clonmel and Down Royal; shortlisted

runners.name: Love Like This
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 41 starts but solid effort over hurdles at Sligo (2m4f, good) nine days ago when staying on dourly to take second; each-way claims if she can back that up.
runners.comment: Still a maiden after 41 starts but solid effort over hurdles at Sligo nine days ago

runners.name: Without Exception
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner; placed twice from five starts over hurdles last season, with a moderate fourth here when last seen; struggled in two chase runs in 2024.
runners.comment: Placed twice from five starts over hurdles last season; struggled in 2 chase runs in 2024

runners.name: La Tene
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward over hurdles when third at Clonmel in March (2m3f, soft); well below that over fences at Tramore next time; headway into midfield when falling two out in Kilbeggan hurdle latest.
runners.comment: Fair effort over hurdles at Clonmel in March but there isn't much to get excited about
runners.owner: John Patrick Ryan & Miss Gillian Ryan

runners.name: Oneforgonzo
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner; gained first chase success at Killarney (20.5f, good to yielding) when keeping on well to score by 0.5l; now 7lb higher but this represents an ease in grade and he's ground versatile.
runners.comment: First chase win at Killarney 10 days ago; 7lb higher but this is an ease in grade; player

runners.name: Malton Groove
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden went close at Wexford last year off 9lb higher but pulled up last two outings; leap of faith required but handicapper is giving him a chance at least.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden went close at Wexford last year off 9lb higher but pulled up last two

runners.name: Cullenwaine
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-19 over fences; lost his form for Philip Rothwell last summer but two of his three runs for this yard full of promise, on latest when runner-up to an easy winner at Tramore; contender.
runners.comment: Runner-up to an easy winner at Tramore last time; contender from unchanged mark

runners.name: Uptaurintop
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best efforts have come at this track over hurdles, headed on the run-in when third here last month (2m); pulled up sole point outing; stamina to prove on chase debut.
runners.comment: Fair effort here latest; pulled up sole point outing; stamina to prove on chase debut

runners.name: Fr Gilligansvoyge
runners.spotlight: Just the one success from 68 starts under rules when landing the Bishopscourt Cup at Punchestown in 2023; good fourth in that race last month when sent off 100-1 but up against it here.
runners.comment: One win from 68 starts under rules when landing the 2023 Bishopscourt Cup at Punchestown

runners.name: My Cullen
runners.spotlight: Maiden posted fair runs in bumpers/hurdles for Henrietta Knight; made good late headway on second chase start at Down Royal (23.5f, good) 13 days ago but held in third when fell two out; yard had a winner at Wexford on Saturday; not discounted.
runners.comment: Fell last time but made good late headway; yard had a winner on Saturday; in the mix

runners.name: A Year Older
runners.spotlight: Won Fairyhouse handicap hurdle (3m2f, yielding) in January and was in contention over the same trip when falling four out over fences at Down Royal last time; raised 4lb for that.
runners.comment: Easy win over hurdles and in contention when a faller over fences last time; progressive

runners.name: Stormy Master
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner; scored at Wexford last summer off 85; well beaten off much lower hurdles mark at Tipperary in October; veteran returns from 227-day absence; may need the run.
runners.comment: Four-time chase winner; scored at Wexford last summer off 85; may need the run here

runners.name: Kilkee Bay
runners.spotlight: Ran some okay races for Gordon Elliott but yet to fire since joining this stable; back from lengthy break when unseated rider at the last (sixth at the time) on Killarney chase debut.
runners.comment: In sixth when unseated rider at Killarney 10 days ago behind Oneforgonzo; local trainer
runners.owner: Aiden Larkin & John Larkin & Paudie Dowling & Kenneth Fox

runners.name: Pure Decent
runners.spotlight: Point winner; very lightly raced veteran; handicap chase debut; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Point winner; very lightly raced veteran; handicap chase debut; hard to fancy

races.race_name: Bet With Tote On Racing & Sports Handicap Chase

runners.name: Smallcraftwarning
runners.spotlight: Won at Killarney last July off 12lb lower; respectable run in Cork National (3m4f, gd-yld) off 106 (3lb wrong) last November; pulled up back there three weeks later; out of depth in Killarney National on return from break latest; these are calmer waters and could rebound.
runners.comment: 8lb wrong and outclassed in Killarney National latest, this easier, could rebound

runners.name: Aclass
runners.spotlight: 1-8 over fences, the win coming in a beginners' at Tramore in November (21.5f, soft); fine effort over the same C&D latest, beaten a neck by Nettuno Italia and 3lb better off now; ran respectably over 21.5f here in April, shaping like this trip could suit; rain no problem; in the mix.
runners.comment: Tramore beginners' winner, 3lb swing with Nettuno Italia for neck defeat latest, player

runners.name: Nettuno Italia
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over fences at Tramore (21.5f, yielding) on latest, beating Aclass by a neck; 3lb worse off with that rival now but yard in form and he won't have any issues if the ground turns testing (won over hurdles on heavy); should go well again.
runners.comment: Beat Aclass by a neck on latest, up 6lb, yard in form, softer ground fine, go well again

runners.name: Attaboyarchie
runners.spotlight: 0-28 under rules and 0-8 in chases but has run well in defeat on occasion in this sphere; decent fourth at Leopardstown (21.5f, yielding) in March; 8l second at Down Royal (3m2f, good) in April; modest fifth at Perth latest; needs to find more to get his head in front.
runners.comment: Maiden, 0-8 over fences, has run respectably in defeat, needs more to get head in front

runners.name: Neon Diamond
runners.spotlight: 1-7 over hurdles and rated 117; earned an opening chase mark of 115 and could only manage a 25l fifth of 10 in first handicap in this sphere at Down Royal (3m2f, good); eased 2lb; best hurdles form on yielding to soft and heavy so rain would enhance her prospects.
runners.comment: 1-7 over hurdles, beaten off 115 in first h'cap chase, may do better if rain arrives

runners.name: Dorans Law
runners.spotlight: Decisive winner of handicap chase at Wexford (25.5f, good) last July off 4lb lower; pulled up both starts since returning from a break, in the Ulster National and over the banks at Punchestown; this more suitable but others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Won off 4lb lower last July, pulled up twice since back from break, others in better form

runners.name: Nastya
runners.spotlight: Killarney beginners' chase winner (23.5f, yielding); 100-30 favourite on handicap chase debut at Down Royal in November (3m, gd-yld) and was under pressure but in contention when falling two out (Battle Of Mirbat won); off since and returns with yard in hot form; ground versatile and could go well.
runners.comment: Off since falling two out when fav' at Down Royal in Nov', yard in form, go well if ready

runners.name: Nytol
runners.spotlight: Handicap chase debutante; 2-10 over hurdles (both wins on good ground) and rated 4lb higher in that sphere; not many positives to take from three beginners' chase runs on soft at 15.5f-2m2f; this trip more suitable but needs the forecast rain to miss.
runners.comment: Handicap chase debutante, rated 4lb higher over hurdles, needs forecast rain to miss

runners.name: Harbour Highway
runners.spotlight: Won off 7lb lower at Leopardstown (21.5f, yielding) in March for this rider; pulled up at Fairyhouse on penultimate but back on track when a 4l second of 14 at Kilbeggan latest; looked as though further would suit last time and he's interesting upped in trip; rain would be a negative.
runners.comment: Leopardstown winner in March, shaped like this trip might suit on latest, rain a worry

runners.name: Hascoeur Clermont
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase winner hasn't got his head in front since November 2023; hasn't looked like ending the dry spell in two starts for this stable; beaten 54l at Killarney latest; cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: On long losing run, no impact in two runs for this stable, cheekpieces now tried

runners.name: Battle Of Mirbat
runners.spotlight: Gained third chase win off 5lb lower at Down Royal in November; some decent runs in defeat since including when a 12.5l fourth of 13 at Leopardstown on latest (28.5f, yld-sft); this trip more suitable; place shout but may need more help from the handicapper to get his head back in front.
runners.comment: Three-time chase winner, 5lb above last success, trip too far latest, may be on tough mark

runners.name: Supreme Gift
runners.spotlight: 134-rated hurdler; 1-8 in chases and rated 23lb lower in this sphere but hasn't yet been able to exploit that lower rating in four handicap chase runs for this yard; a bit better in the Killarney National (3m2f, gd-yld) on latest and this is easier; rain fine and can't discount if builds on that for local yard.
runners.comment: Rated 23lb higher over hurdles, a bit better in Killarney National latest, can't discount

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Arth's Gold
runners.spotlight: A few near-misses around here before finally off the mark three weeks ago off 6lb lower (boasts a much better record on Tapeta than Polytrack); holds obvious claims under the vastly experienced Simon Walker.
runners.comment: Few near-misses around here before off the mark three weeks ago off 6lb lower; shortlisted

runners.name: He's Our Cracker
runners.spotlight: Cheekpieces had a positive effect early in the year and he switches to a first-time hood tonight off the back of a below-par effort at Leicester; should be happier back on the AW under Freddie Robson, who's currently a combined 2-7 under both codes.
runners.comment: Switches from cheekpieces to a hood; should be happier back on the AW; each-way shout

runners.name: St Just In Time
runners.spotlight: Off for more than a year since narrowly beaten over hurdles on his debut for his new jumps-oriented yard last March; that came after almost a year off, so he can go well fresh; market useful under a jockey who's ridden two winners over hurdles this year.
runners.comment: Went close over hurdles when last seen; market useful back from over a year off

runners.name: Sisterandbrother
runners.spotlight: Hit traffic when not beaten far over C&D late last month and duly justified favouritism at Doncaster on Saturday; no penalty for that apprentice race win and is shortlisted under the experienced Fletcher Yarham.
runners.comment: No penalty for recent apprentice race win; solid claims under experienced Fletcher Yarham

runners.name: Green Team
runners.spotlight: On the downgrade in recent times for Ian Williams, from whom he was picked up for 7,500gns last month; just a fair debut for his new yard recently and this trip is a bare minimum these days.
runners.comment: Just a fair debut for his new yard recently and this trip is a bare minimum these days

runners.name: It's Only Fun
runners.spotlight: Handicapper quick to ease off this spring and he turned in his best effort for this yard when fourth behind Sisterandbrother at Doncaster last weekend; inclined to over-race back in cheekpieces there and has to prove he can do it on the AW.
runners.comment: Best run for this yard when fourth to Sisterandbrother at the weekend; has to built on it

runners.name: Wrist Art
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who hasn't won since 2023 and never been this far (tried over 6f-1m2f by his current trainer); one of his better efforts from 6lb out of the weights at Windsor ten days ago, but is 6lb wrong again under Ruby Criscuolo (0-2 this far).
runners.comment: Hasn't won since 2023 and never been this far; again runs from 6lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Olivia Jane
runners.spotlight: Rare Flat runner for this, her third yard, back from nine months off; string of poor efforts last year and couldn't be backed unless there's serious support going up in trip.
runners.comment: String of poor efforts last year; couldn't be backed for new yard unless there's support

runners.name: Freewheelin
runners.spotlight: A lot of years since his trainer saddled a Flat winner and he'll do very well to end the run, back from almost a year off and from 6lb out of the handicap under Venetia Thompson (0-7 under both codes currently).
runners.comment: Returns from almost a year off and will do well to figure from 6lb out of the handicap

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap

runners.name: Shalaa Asker
runners.spotlight: Three wins in the winter, including over C&D, and he has continued to run well off his revised mark; drawn wide on turf last time and usually gives his running round here, so holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Drawn wide on turf last time; usually gives his running here, so holds each-way claims

runners.name: Peregrine Falcon
runners.spotlight: Had 13 cracks at it now and enjoyed the run of the race in a small-field maiden when last seen three months ago; that's been boosted since but, while she's consistent, she's fully exposed.
runners.comment: Had 13 cracks at it now; while she's consistent, she's fully exposed; returns from a break

runners.name: Westgate Warrior
runners.spotlight: Confirmed his liking for Nottingham, where he won last autumn, when back-to-form second 12 days ago, again from the front; went close on Tapeta too around the turn of the year and enters calculations.
runners.comment: Went close on Tapeta around the turn of the year; plenty of toe; enters calculations

runners.name: Cayman Tai
runners.spotlight: Still to win a handicap and has been turned over as favourite four times this year; did well to finish second last time (first run under Seamus Cronin) having reared at the start and it's bound to fall right at some point, maybe tonight.
runners.comment: Did well to finish second last time; it's bound to fall right at some point, maybe tonight

runners.name: Toomuchforme
runners.spotlight: Wildly inconsistent filly who's finished well held a few times since springing a 33-1 shock over C&D in March; it seems the positive early impact of the visor may have worn off.
runners.comment: Wildly inconsistent; well held a few times since springing a 33-1 shock over C&D in March

runners.name: Man On A Mission
runners.spotlight: Drawn wide back up to 6f last time and that run is best overlooked; previously in good form over C&D, winning at a short price late last month in 0-55 company, but this race is stronger and he's 6lb higher.
runners.comment: Won at a short price over C&D last month in 0-55 company; 6lb higher now in stronger race

runners.name: Popular Dream
runners.spotlight: Yard quiet so far this year and this C&D regular has been given a break since below par here in March; on a career-low mark now, 4lb below his last winning figure, but needs a revival.
runners.comment: On a career-low mark now, 4lb below his last winning figure, but needs a revival

runners.name: Hover On The Wind
runners.spotlight: Boasts a decent record around this time of year; below par on turf last time but he's bounced back from blips before and he's in off the same mark as when going close over C&D in March; one to consider.
runners.comment: Has the same mark as when going close over C&D in March; one to consider

runners.name: J Street
runners.spotlight: Just a maiden win to her name and has often pulled hard, including a couple of times for her current yard; fair comeback at Doncaster in March, albeit from a good draw, and is worth considering.
runners.comment: Fair comeback at Doncaster in March, albeit from a good draw, and is worth considering

runners.name: Fantasy Obsessor
runners.spotlight: Began handicap life on an unrealistic mark last year having won her first two starts in novice company; competitive again now, though, finishing a solid second at Wolverhampton when down to 5f on Monday in first-time cheekpieces; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Competitive again now, finishing second at Wolverhampton when down to 5f on Monday; player

races.race_name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mehmas Champion
runners.spotlight: Showed some good form in Germany a year ago including when fourth in their 2,000 Guineas (1m, good; Group 2), but well held on stable debut at Redcar (1m, good) a fortnight ago; may have needed the run after a year off, but he does need to step forward.
runners.comment: Well held on stable debut at Redcar a fortnight ago; may have needed the run

runners.name: King Of Thebes
runners.spotlight: 1,500,000gns yearling; third in both starts over longer trips last month including over 1m3f here, but was well behind the winner on each occasion; little to suggest dropping in trip will help, particularly on pedigree.
runners.comment: Third in both starts over further last month; little to suggest dropping in trip will help

runners.name: Golden Buddy
runners.spotlight: 180,000euros breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to four winners including Cabo Spirit (US 1m-1m2f turf including 2yo/Grade 2; RPR 118) and Convention (US 7f-1m1f dirt); dam 6f 2yo winner (68), out of US 6.5f Grade 2 winning sister to dual Breeders' Cup Classic winner Tiznow; appealing pedigree and interesting to see how he fares in the market, especially compared to his stablemates.
runners.comment: Appealing pedigree and interesting to see how he fares in the market

runners.name: Intsaar
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam placed 8.5f-1m2f in France/US, sister to US stakes winners Artemus Kitten (8.5f), Redwood Kitten (1m) and Lisa's Kitten (1m), is granddaughter of half-sister to US 1m/1m1f Grade 1 winner Scan; enough to like on breeding and the market should prove informative.
runners.comment: Enough to like on breeding and the market should prove informative

runners.name: Affettuoso
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut when beaten less than a length by a subsequent winner at Wolverhampton (9.5f, Tapeta) in December; the drop back in trip may not be totally ideal, but he is still shortlisted in this field; stable runs four.
runners.comment: Beaten less than a length by a subsequent winner at Wolverhampton in December; shortlisted

runners.name: Kahin
runners.spotlight: 240,000gns foal; sent off 13-2 and looked in need of the experience when seventh of 11 on his Windsor debut (1m, good to firm) 17 days ago (a length behind Mostaan); open to improvement and worth a second look.
runners.comment: Looked in need of the experience on his Windsor debut; open to improvement

runners.name: Renovator
runners.spotlight: 14,000gns foal, 32,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Ertebat (6f; RPR 84) and Kamekist (6f AW; 78); dam 7f AW winner (79), out of 6f Listed winning half-sister to Group 3 winners Cupid's Glory (7f 2yo) and Clinical (1m1f); already gelded and probably best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Already gelded and probably best watched unless the market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Eljowhary
runners.spotlight: 140,000gns yearling; sent off 13-2 when beaten less than 3l into sixth of ten on his Newcastle debut (7f, Tapeta) this month; open to improvement, but Affettuoso looks the stable's main hope.
runners.comment: Sixth of ten on his Newcastle debut this month; open to improvement

runners.name: Levens Hall
runners.spotlight: 130,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Dan (Listed-placed 1m including 2yo; RPR 102), Mokaatil (prolific 5f-6f including 2yo/AW; 101), Dream Dreamer (5f 2yo; 82), Meredif (5f 2yo; 78) and See You Tonight (6f AW; 78); dam Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner (98); something to like on breeding, but already gelded and apprentice-ridden on debut.
runners.comment: Something to like on breeding, but already gelded and apprentice-ridden on debut

runners.name: Mostaan
runners.spotlight: Fifth in his first two starts seven months apart; looks one for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Fifth in his first two starts seven months apart; looks one for handicaps after this

runners.name: Sunnie Jim
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to four winners including Almosh'her (1m/1m4f including AW; RPR 103), Pappa Louis (7f 2yo; 78) and Love Yourself (Italian 6.7f-1m3f); dam Listed-placed Italian 1m1f-1m3f winner (95); already gelded and handicaps over further may be his thing in due course.
runners.comment: Already gelded and handicaps over further may be his thing in due course

runners.name: Harmonics
runners.spotlight: Sent off 14-1 and showed ability alongside greenness when fifth of 12 on his debut over C&D last month; bred to need further in time, but still worth considering if coming on from that first effort; stable also runs the newcomer Levens Hall.
runners.comment: Showed ability alongside greenness when fifth of 12 on his debut over C&D last month

runners.name: Lone Warrior
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty of ability in two starts over 1m on turf for Andrew Balding last summer, the form of both races working out extremely well; changed hands for 85,000gns soon afterwards and makes his debut for his new yard after 11 months off having been gelded and with the hood removed; out of an AW winner; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Plenty of ability in two starts for Andrew Balding last summer; worth a market check

races.race_name: Follow @attheraces On X Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Royal Fanfare
runners.spotlight: 5-4 favourite when second of 12 on Kempton debut (1m, Polytrack) three weeks ago, beaten less than a length and finishing clear of the third, with Lightning Glory in fourth; that was still a good effort from a wide draw and she looks very much the one to beat.
runners.comment: Beaten just under a length on her Kempton debut and clear of the rest; the one to beat

runners.name: Dream Camp
runners.spotlight: 250,000euros yearling; promise in both starts over 7f, including when third of seven at Salisbury (good to firm) 18 days ago; handicaps are an option after this but she's still one for the shortlist in this field with the extra furlong likely to suit.
runners.comment: Promise in two starts over 7f; one for the shortlist with the extra furlong likely to suit

runners.name: Lightning Glory
runners.spotlight: 165,000euros breeze-up 2yo; sent off 17-2 when fourth of 12 on her Kempton debut (1m, Polytrack) three weeks ago, finishing just over 3l behind Royal Fanfare; open to improvement but it will be needed.
runners.comment: Fourth on her Kempton debut three weeks ago, finishing just over 3l behind Royal Fanfare
runners.owner: Duaij Al Khalifa & Ahmad Al Qassimi

runners.name: Blue Tulip
runners.spotlight: 28,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Contact (1m-1m4f including 2yo; RPR 103), Just Hubert (1m2f-2m4f including 2yo; 98) and Burst Of Colour (Japanese 6f/7f); dam French 1m-1m5f winner (including AW) out of Group-placed 6f/1m 2yo winner; makes some appeal on pedigree and the market should indicate what is expected.
runners.comment: Makes some appeal on pedigree and the market should indicate what is expected

runners.name: Halimede
runners.spotlight: 65,000euros foal; 85,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners Rahmi (7f/1m; RPR 115), Rayounpour (1m2f-1m4f including KSA/Bahrain; 98) and Razdan (1m turf/1m4f AW, 84; 2m3f hurdle); dam 1m3f-1m4f winner (88), half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Raydara; something to like on breeding and she's worth a market check, but hooded on debut and may need further in due course.
runners.comment: Something to like on breeding, but may need further in due course; hood on

runners.name: South Beach
runners.spotlight: 10,000euros foal; half-sister to winners Steppingstone (Czech 6.5f/7f) and Sabalan (German 1m4f); dam German 1m2f winner, closely related to French 1m6f Listed winner Sun Of Gold, is granddaughter of German Oaks winner; bred to need further and best watched on debut unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Bred to need further and best watched on debut unless the market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Brunhilde
runners.spotlight: Closely related to winners Ghostwatch (1m4f-1m6f including AW/Listed; RPR 111) and Forever Be (1m4f; 99), half-sister to three winners including Pure Nature (1m4f AW; 91) and Nature's Order (1m4f-2m1f including AW); dam 1m2f-1m4f winner (including Listed; 100); attractive pedigree and stable's newcomers are always worthy of respect, but her breeding does suggest she will come into her own over further.
runners.comment: Attractive pedigree but her breeding does suggest she will come into her own over further

runners.name: Regal Desire
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns yearling; half-sister to UAE 9.5f dirt winner Withering (RPR 109); dam unraced sister to 5.5f-1m winner Predetermined, half-sister to winners Classic Blade (6f 2yo Group 2), Raadobarg (smart 7f-1m) and Captain Dunne (smart 5f); something to like on breeding, but stable doesn't have many winning newcomers.
runners.comment: There's something to like on breeding but her stable doesn't have many winning newcomers

runners.name: Sound Gloria
runners.spotlight: 16-1 when beating one home on her debut over C&D in November; needs a transformation after six months off.
runners.comment: Beat one home on her debut over C&D in November (16-1); needs a transformation

runners.name: Hunting Berries
runners.spotlight: 150-1 when a well-beaten last of 12 on her debut over 6f here last September.
runners.comment: 150-1 when a well-beaten last of 12 on her debut over 6f here last September

runners.name: Topathemorning
runners.spotlight: 80-1 but did hint at ability when sixth of ten on her Newmarket debut (7f, good to firm) last September; it's likely she will need further than this before she comes into her own, though.
runners.comment: Hinted at ability on Newmarket debut last September but it's likely she will need further

races.race_name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands A, B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Parioca
runners.spotlight: 15,000gns daughter of Cotai Glory and the first foal of a once-raced (finished last)) half-sister to three winners; wears cheekpieces on debut; other make greater paper appeal.
runners.comment: Wears cheekpieces on debut and others make more obvious paper appeal

runners.name: Cash Cove
runners.spotlight: 36,000gns half-sister to winners By The Book (Group-placed 7f-1m4f including 2yo/AW; RPR 112) and Cash Out (1m including  AW/France; 97); dam unraced half-sister to 1m1f Group 2 winner Lord Admiral; makes appeal on breeding for a trainer who got off the mark with 2yos in 2026 at Ripon on Sunday.
runners.comment: Makes paper appeal for yard which got off mark for the year with 2yos at Ripon on Sunday

runners.name: Guadalevin
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns half-sister to winners Crema Inglesa (1m-1m2f; RPR 90), Hilts (1m2f-12.4f; 85), Point In Time (1m4f including AW; 84), Abbey Actress (1m2f/1m3f; 84) and Creme Chantilly (1m AW including 2yo; 75); dam 1m2f winner (97); the stable's 2yos have been going well, particularly on AW, so she rates a likely type, all the more so if backed.
runners.comment: The stable's 2yos have been going well, particularly on AW, so she rates a likely type

runners.name: Sea Idol
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns gelded son of Sea The Moon; first foal of an unraced half-sister to 9.5f winner Sultanov Swing; stable can ready them but a watching brief is the percentage call with its first timers.
runners.comment: Gelded son of Sea The Moon; a watching brief is advised unless betting hints otherwise

runners.name: Choreography
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns half-brother to winners Citronnade (1m3f/1m4f; RPR 98) and Ardenlee Star (6f-1m including 2yo/US dirt; 83); dam 1m-1m4f winner (including US; 90); one to note in the market.
runners.comment: One to note in the market on debut for yard which can ready one
runners.owner: J P M O'Connor & Edward J Ware

runners.name: Crown Ranger
runners.spotlight: 58,000euros son of Camelot who is the first foal of 5f-7f maiden (RPR 67); the stable had a 2yo win easily over this trip at Lingfield on Monday so market confidence behind this one would look significant.
runners.comment: Yard has easy 2yo winner over this trip on Monday; market support significant

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Fort Augustus
runners.spotlight: Finally off the mark over C&D in a first-time cheekpieces/second-time hood combination last month; didn't look to have any excuses back here when favourite off this mark a fortnight ago but this is weaker if anything.
runners.comment: Didn't look to have any excuses as favourite over C&D latest but this weaker if anything

runners.name: Just King High
runners.spotlight: Regressive for Thomas Faulkner in various forms of headgear before switching to this yard in early spring; pulled hard on turf last time and returns to cheekpieces back up from 6f.
runners.comment: Pulled hard on turf last time and returns to cheekpieces back up from 6f; needs more

runners.name: Adelaide Bay
runners.spotlight: Struggled for two yards in recent times; perhaps the return to Jane Chapple-Hyam, for whom he won three times last season, will do the trick down another 3lb.
runners.comment: Maybe the return to this yard, for whom he won three times last season, will do the trick

runners.name: Billyjoegold
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a few winners, including the yard's prolific Bobby Joe Leg; offered a bit of promise on debut before two poor efforts (all 7f at Newcastle); would want to see some support now handicapping.
runners.comment: Bit of promise on debut before two poor efforts; would want to see some support

runners.name: My Mate Beattie
runners.spotlight: Yard, who also run Lil Wade, ended a losing run early in the week; this longstanding maiden, who hasn't achieved much in four runs since leaving Ed Dunlop, isn't obviously fancied.
runners.comment: Not achieved much in four runs since leaving Ed Dunlop; isn't obviously fancied

runners.name: Lil Wade
runners.spotlight: Poor maiden who's done most racing in sprints and not looked to see it out in a handful of starts over this trip; two poor efforts this spring leave him hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Poor maiden who's done most racing in sprints; hard to recommend

runners.name: Amaysmont
runners.spotlight: Winless in 18 months and has regressed in recent times; quite happy on Tapeta but most wins have come after a recent outing and he's been off since a poor one on turf in March.
runners.comment: Winless in 18 months and regressed in recent times; off since a poor one on turf in March

runners.name: Nubough
runners.spotlight: Fine on Tapeta but all his wins have come on Polytrack, including twice last spring at Chelmsford; still dangerous when dipping into this company but he's unpredictable now and 7f round here is enough of a test.
runners.comment: Still dangerous at this level but unpredictable now and 7f round here is enough of a test

runners.name: Highfield Jewel
runners.spotlight: Bit of promise behind a couple of subsequent winners in a maiden here early in the year; finished a remote last of six on turf back from a break recently, though, and needs to settle better back up from 6f; market useful.
runners.comment: Remote last of six on turf latest; needs to settle better back up from 6f; market useful

races.race_name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: South Kensington
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f winner for Martin Dunne last year and ran her best race for this yard when second of 13 at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) ten days ago, meeting trouble turning in yet still finishing well clear of the third; major player from the same mark (3lb well in) if enjoying a bit more luck.
runners.comment: Best race for this yard when second of 13 at Wolverhampton ten days ago; major player

runners.name: Sanditon
runners.spotlight: Not at his best lately, but 4lb lower than when gaining his sole success at Chelmsford (7f, Polytrack) in February; no great surprise if he bounces back at some point.
runners.comment: 4lb lower than when gaining his sole success at Chelmsford in February; could bounce back

runners.name: King Sharja
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who has dropped 1lb below his last winning mark, but would like to have seen a bit more from him on last month's reappearance back here.
runners.comment: C&D winner; 1lb below last winning mark, but well held on last month's reappearance here

runners.name: Turbo Command
runners.spotlight: Losing run up to 14, but three of his five wins have come over C&D and he performed much better when second of 11 back here a fortnight ago; a player from the same mark.
runners.comment: Three wins over C&D and back to form when second here last time; a player from same mark

runners.name: The Cutest
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden who finished about 5l behind Turbo Command over C&D a fortnight ago; switch from a hood to a visor will need to make a big difference.
runners.comment: 0-13; switch from a hood to a visor will need to make a big difference

runners.name: Megaphone
runners.spotlight: Continues to edge down the weights, but 0-16 in handicaps and stamina for this far remains a major concern; finished behind two of these over C&D a fortnight ago; stable also runs Vecchio.
runners.comment: Continues to edge down the weights, but stamina for this far remains a major concern

runners.name: Vecchio
runners.spotlight: Long time since he has shown any worthwhile form and now 0-28; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Long time since he has shown any worthwhile form and now 0-28

runners.name: Lowestoft
runners.spotlight: No closer than sixth in five starts over 6f-1m on the AW for Christine Dunnett; makes his stable debut (still owned by his former trainer) in a first-time tongue-tie after four months off; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No closer than sixth in five starts; tongue-tie on for stable debut; hard to fancy

runners.name: No News
runners.spotlight: Went close here (6f) last September, but losing run up to 20 and he may need this after 227 days off.
runners.comment: Went close here last September, but losing run up to 20; may need this after 227 days off

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap

runners.name: Forever Noah
runners.spotlight: Can make the running; twice runner-up at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) this spring and had excuses for a lesser effort over 5f there last time; each-way possibilities.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up at Wolverhampton this spring and had excuses for a lesser effort last time

runners.name: Iconic Times
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come at Kempton (6f, Polytrack), but he has mainly been racing over further this year; quiet in first two starts for this yard (1m/7f) and although the return to this trip may suit and he drops into a Class 6 for the first time, others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Return to this trip may suit and he drops in grade, but others are more compelling

runners.name: Big Apple Jack
runners.spotlight: 0-10, but made a promising return from 181 days off when second of seven at Nottingham (6f, good) last month; each-way claims if as effective on AW debut.
runners.comment: 0-10 but ran well on last month's return; each-way claims if as effective on AW debut

runners.name: Albert Cee
runners.spotlight: Didn't get home over 7f here in March and is back off the same mark as when winning over C&D in January; off two months, but has won after much longer absences and high on the list.
runners.comment: Back off the same mark as when winning over C&D in January; high on the list

runners.name: Kyoto
runners.spotlight: Has shown some ability in three starts at Lingfield (6f, Polytrack); open to improvement on handicap/Tapeta debut and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Showed ability in three starts at Lingfield; open to improvement on handicap/Tapeta debut

runners.name: Bomb Squad
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner, but has raced exclusively over 7f since December and was successful over that distance here four weeks ago; has to prove he still has the speed for this trip, but his course record makes him hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Has to prove he still has the speed for 6f but his course record makes him hard to dismiss

runners.name: Watermelon Sugar
runners.spotlight: In good form at Lingfield during the winter and is a bit of specialist over 6f there (112121), but he has won over this C&D; latest Kempton effort not hard to forgive given his record at that track and could easily bounce back; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Has won over C&D and latest Kempton effort not hard to forgive; could easily bounce back

runners.name: Mu Mu Land
runners.spotlight: Yet to win a handicap, but was having his second start since wind surgery when second of ten over C&D last month; races off the same mark and considered.
runners.comment: Second of ten over C&D last month; races off the same mark and considered

runners.name: Tommy McJohn
runners.spotlight: 1-16, the win coming in a Naas nursery (5f, good) as a 2yo; may have needed his stable debut at Ayr (5f, good to firm) 16 days ago and worth a market check off his much lower AW mark.
runners.comment: 1-16; may have needed his stable debut and worth a market check off his much lower AW mark

runners.name: Daytona Lady
runners.spotlight: Running well both here and at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) since returning last month and has twice won over this C&D in her career; major player, especially if getting a solid pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Running well since returning last month; has won twice over C&D; major player
runners.owner: Eric Broadwith And Mrs Ruth Carr

runners.name: One More Dream
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (6f/7f) and 3lb lower than when successful at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) last September, but he can't be entertained on his performances in the meantime.
runners.comment: 3lb below last winning mark, but can't be entertained on his recent performances

races.race_name: LSL Racing Horse Sales Syndication Leasing Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (GBB/IRE Incentive)

runners.name: Ariane Sky
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and she stuck to her task when fifth of ten at Bath (5.5f, firm; Turtle Star last) three weeks ago; candidate to build on that quite promising introduction.
runners.comment: 25-1 and fifth (5.5f, firm) 3 weeks ago; could build on that quite promising introduction

runners.name: Forest Berry
runners.spotlight: 11-1 and she showed a bit when seventh at Kempton (6f, AW) three weeks ago; will probably do better over further but still no surprise if she can improve upon that effort this time.
runners.comment: 11-1 and showed a bit when seventh (6f, AW) 3 weeks ago; might build on that

runners.name: Romidijo
runners.spotlight: Flashed her tail but it wasn't a bad first attempt when eighth of 12 at Ascot (5f, good; 16-1) a fortnight ago; kept on that day so this step up in trip looks a positive and she's respected despite her wing draw possibly not being ideal.
runners.comment: Quite a promising first go; step up to 6f looks a positive; respected despite wing draw

runners.name: Alfey Moon
runners.spotlight: March foal who is a half-brother to 5f winner Bust A Moon (RPR 71); dam 5f 2yo winner (93), sister to Greek 6f-1m winner Touch Paper, closely related to 5.5f/7f winner Limelite, half-sister to 7f winner Swindon; encouraging his dam won on her May juvenile debut, so it will be interesting to see how he goes in the market, especially compared to stablemate My A'Ali Baba.
runners.comment: March foal; dam won on her May juvenile debut; watch for market interest on this first go

runners.name: Jazzy Bay
runners.spotlight: April foal and 10,000gns yearling; dam unplaced 6f-1m2f (RPR 38), closely related to 9.4f Group 3 winner/Irish Oaks runner-up Jack Naylor, out of 1m2f Listed winner; stable off the mark with its juveniles and always to be feared in this sort of event; market helpful.
runners.comment: April foal and 10,000gns yearling; stable always to be feared in this sort of event

runners.name: Metamouse
runners.spotlight: 8,000euros March foal, 18,000euros yearling; dam 7f/1m winner (RPR 71), sister to Listed-placed 1m winner Miss Sienna, out of 1m2f winning half-sister to 7f/1m Group 3 winner Cheyenne Star; watch for market confidence ahead of this introduction.
runners.comment: 8,000euros March foal, 18,000euros yearling; watch for market confidence on his debut

runners.name: Issy's Gift
runners.spotlight: 19,000euros April foal and 27,000gns yearling; thirteenth foal; half-brother to six winners including Galeron (Group-placed 6f/7f 2yo; RPR 109), Goldenfield (1m2f/1m4f; 89) and Red Icon (6f 2yo; 87); dam unraced half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 3 winner Good Mood; makes paper appeal for a yard that's off the mark with its juveniles.
runners.comment: 19,000euros April foal and 27,000gns yearling; makes paper appeal on his debut

runners.name: Benefacta
runners.spotlight: Late February foal and 5,500gns yearling; already-gelded half-brother to winners Betties Bay (6f including 2yo; RPR 85), Bobacious (1m2f; 79) and Colinski (11.6f; 64); dam 6f-1m winner (including 2yo; 83), half-sister to useful 7f/1m winner Loving Spirit, out of 1m3f/1m4f winner; stable ticking along nicely with its 2yos and betting should provide some clues ahead of this one's debut.
runners.comment: Late February foal and 5,500gns yearling; betting to provide clues ahead of his debut

runners.name: Green Titan
runners.spotlight: February foal and £20,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Tropical Island (5f-7f including 2yo/Listed; RPR 98), Jimmy Mark (1m2f/11.6f; 62) and Stellar Arrow (Bahraini 1m); dam won three 5f races at two, her only season to race; respected yard and this newcomer is of interest.
runners.comment: February foal and £20,000 yearling; respected yard and this newcomer is of interest

runners.name: My A'Ali Baba
runners.spotlight: March foal; 32,000gns yearling, £23,000 breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to four winners including Mistress Of Venice (5f; RPR 99), Tommy De Vito (6f; 97) and War Of Succession (8.4f/1m2f; 89); dam placed 5.7f (68), half-sister to 7f Listed winner Kalindi; market likely to be a fair guide ahead of this debut.
runners.comment: March foal; 32,000gns yearling, £23,000 breeze-up 2yo; market likely to be a fair guide

runners.name: Marralyn
runners.spotlight: 10-1 from 20-1 but she finished last of five at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) on her introduction one month ago; will need to step forward from that on her first crack at turf.
runners.comment: 10-1 from 20-1 but last of five (5f, AW) on her introduction last month; turf debut

runners.name: Turtle Star
runners.spotlight: 25-1 and she never figured when last of ten at Bath (5.5f, firm; Ariane Sky fifth) three weeks ago; stable's juveniles can improve for a run but she'll need to.
runners.comment: Last of ten 3 weeks ago; stable's juveniles can improve for a run but she'll need to

races.race_name: Why Bet Elsewhere, David Lucas Bookmakers Handicap

runners.name: Em Four
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins (6f and 5f) from his last five starts and running well on turf too; not beaten far when third of five at Salisbury (5f, good to firm) this month; player if this forecast slower ground is no issue.
runners.comment: Two AW wins (6f and 5f) from his last five starts and running well on turf too

runners.name: So Smart
runners.spotlight: C&D (good to soft) winner in August 2024 who won at Lingfield (good to firm) and Ffos Las (again 5f, good) last year; couldn't repeat his win in that Lingfield event 12 days ago and this looks a warmer race than the ones he regularly contests; still not dismissed.
runners.comment: C&D winner but this is warmer than the events he regularly contests; still not ruled out

runners.name: El Bufalo
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner who was having just his second crack at a turf handicap when good sixth of 17 at Ascot (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago; also switched across from a poor draw to deliver his bid so that effort can probably be upgraded a little further; forecast softer ground is an unknown but otherwise respected.
runners.comment: Good sixth of 17 at Ascot (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago; unexposed on turf; player

runners.name: Lazzar
runners.spotlight: Landed his fourth AW race when scoring narrowly at Kempton (6f) in March; has dipped below that winning mark after three lesser efforts and blinkers go on for his eighth race on turf (0-7).
runners.comment: Won a fourth AW race (6f) in March; mark fine but 0-7 on turf; blinkers go on

runners.name: Betsen
runners.spotlight: Turf winner (5f, good) in Ireland who scored at Southwell (6f, AW) on his stable debut in November; that 40-1 success hasn't been built on but this represents a drop back in grade; not confidently ruled out.
runners.comment: 40-1 stable debut win hasn't been built on but this represents a drop back in grade

runners.name: Marching Mac
runners.spotlight: Seven turf wins on ground ranging from good to heavy; not in peak form on AW this year but last autumn's wins at Bath (5.5f, heavy) and Leicester (5f, soft) make him of firm interest off this mark.
runners.comment: Not in peak form on AW this year but last autumn's wins make him of firm interest

races.race_name: Guaranteed Odds At David Lucas Bookmakers Handicap

runners.name: Boston Max
runners.spotlight: Gelded after three qualifying runs and it was a big jolt of improvement when scoring at Dundalk (5f, AW; first-time tongue-tie) on his handicap debut four weeks ago; 7lb rise but he did that cosily and could start rising through the ranks.
runners.comment: Big improvement when scoring (5f, AW; first-time tongue-tie) on handicap debut; key player

runners.name: Jane Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Tends to lead; 0-10 and consistency has been an issue but she went close on AW at Southwell (5f) in March and gave it another good go when third back there (6f) three weeks ago; makes first start on turf since her 2yo debut.
runners.comment: Went close (5f) in March and solid third (6f) latest; makes first turf start since debut

runners.name: Forever Glamorous
runners.spotlight: Gelded over the winter and it was a fine start for Christopher Mason when neck second of 13 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) this month; likely has the pace to cope with this drop back to 5f and he's firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Good yard debut when neck second (6f, good to firm) this month; likely has the pace for 5f

runners.name: My Mate Mackley
runners.spotlight: Barely rateable on his first two starts (1m) but wasn't totally disgraced when dropped down to 6f at Chelmsford for third qualifying run; has been gelded ahead of this turf/handicap debut and any market support should be heeded.
runners.comment: Not totally disgraced at 6f on last qualifying run; gelded ahead of turf/handicap debut

runners.name: Stock Market
runners.spotlight: Possibly not the most straightforward but it was a big run when second of 15 at Salisbury (6f, good; front pair clear) last Thursday; that was a career best and subsequent 3lb nudge could have been worse; previous third over 5f showed this trip is no bother and he's considered.
runners.comment: Career best when second of 15 (6f, good) last week; fine at 5f and should go well

races.race_name: Treble Odds L15s At David Lucas Handicap

runners.name: Romanovich
runners.spotlight: Four-time C&D winner, including twice in the month of May; got his head back in front at Wolverhampton (7f) in March; respectable return to turf when third of 11 at Bath (1m good) 17 days ago; can be expected to go well back at Chepstow.
runners.comment: Four-time C&D winner; fair return to turf at Bath and has to enter calculations back here
runners.owner: Mrs S M Maine & Roger Gould

runners.name: Dapper Gee Gee
runners.spotlight: Better than ever when readily seeing off 11 rivals at Southwell (7f) under Gina Mangan in January; never involved over 1m at Doncaster next time but shaped as if still in form when fourth back at Southwell (7f) 23 days ago.
runners.comment: AW winner under Gina Mangan in January; fair fourth latest; claims

runners.name: Terries Royale
runners.spotlight: His two wins for Michael Appleby came over a stiff 1m on soft ground in autumn 2024 but has a largely regressive profile since; offered a bit more for current yard when fourth at Wolverhampton in March but last of six at Kempton since (both 7f); it remains to be seen whether a return to turf helps.
runners.comment: Has largely struggled since autumn 2024, including for current yard in recent starts

runners.name: Margorie
runners.spotlight: Sprung a surprise in a soft-ground Windsor maiden last September; no impact in handicaps since but the ground has been good to firm back on turf on her last two starts and today's conditions should be a bit closer to those which she encountered when winning; no shock were she to bounce back to form with a bang.
runners.comment: No impact in handicaps since soft-ground maiden win but going may be more in favour now

runners.name: Too Much Trevor
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice here (including 7f) last year but he's still a maiden after 28 starts; well-held fifth on 1m Bath reappearance five weeks ago and it's likely the wait for a first win will go on.
runners.comment: Placed a few times at Chepstow but Bath reappearance fifth took record to 0-28

runners.name: Connie's Rose
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who has gained seven of her ten career wins at Chepstow; went backwards from a sound reappearance effort at Bath (extended 5f) when last of six over a bare 5f there 12 days later but a revival can't be ruled out back at Chepstow; raced exclusively at sprint trips since 2023 but won over C&D in 2022.
runners.comment: Raced over 5f/6f since 2023 but has won over C&D; can't rule out back at Chepstow (7 wins)

runners.name: Opening Bat
runners.spotlight: Made second handicap start a winning one at Kempton (1m) last May; creditable third on next three starts (including 7f) before failing to fire in cheekpieces (which are quickly discarded) on final 3yo outing; reappears with his stable back in form and high on the shortlist under Billy Loughnane.
runners.comment: Solid 3yo campaign & reappears with yard back among winners; player under Billy Loughnane
runners.owner: Mrs R J McCreery And Richard Craddock

runners.name: Stoneywell
runners.spotlight: Fair form when third in a 7f Leicester novice last October but it's a standout run so far, finishing well held in handicaps on all three subsequent outings; the stable also runs Wodao.
runners.comment: Third in novice last October but hasn't come close to reproducing in three handicaps since

runners.name: Sixfiveseven
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m at Newmarket (good; also second on soft) in autumn 2024 but absent for 487 days prior to weakening into ninth of ten at Kempton (1m) three weeks ago; entitled to strip fitter (return to turf may also help) but a leap of faith is needed.
runners.comment: Turf win in autumn 2024 but absent for 15 months prior to well held on AW recently

runners.name: Spirit Of The Bay
runners.spotlight: Took her Chepstow record to 2-4 when seeing off five rivals over 1m2f last July but her form dipped at the end of the year; races over short of 1m for the first time in five years on her reappearance and a watching brief is the call despite her good record here.
runners.comment: 1m2f course win last July made it 2-4 at Chepstow but much shorter trip a worry on return

runners.name: Cypriot Diaspora
runners.spotlight: A dual winner over the extended 7f at Ffos Las (good/soft) last August; made an encouraging return when fifth over 7f at Kempton in March but well held back on turf at Leicester 12 days ago.
runners.comment: Two Ffos Las wins last summer but failed to build on reappearance when well held latest

runners.name: Wodao
runners.spotlight: Tumbled down the weights for Jamie Osborne without really looking like capitalising, but worth a second look in the betting for new yard back from four months off under Clifford Lee who has a very healthy 10-49 record for the stable.
runners.comment: Tumbled in weights without threatening but worth betting check for new yard after break

races.race_name: Get Raceday Ready With Lucas Bookies Handicap

runners.name: Dancing With Drums
runners.spotlight: 3-7 on AW, his latest win coming at Kempton (6f) a fortnight ago on third start for Adam Kirby; up 3lb on just this second go on turf and he can probably do better again.
runners.comment: 3-7 on AW, including at Kempton (6f) latest; second go on turf and he's of clear interest

runners.name: Dayman
runners.spotlight: Two wins at 7f on AW and he went close on that surface at Wolverhampton (7f) in March; below that form on last two starts and has something to prove back at 6f on this forecast easy turf; blinkers go on.
runners.comment: Best form at 7f on AW; something to prove back at 6f on easy turf; blinkers go on

runners.name: Griselda
runners.spotlight: 0-10 for this yard but she went close at Southwell (6f, AW) on her penultimate start; 14l defeat at Yarmouth last time tempers confidence, however.
runners.comment: Went close penultimate start but 14l defeat last time tempers confidence

runners.name: Anglesey Lad
runners.spotlight: 0-21 but he's been placed on a third of those occasions; not disgraced when fourth at Bath (Alyara behind) last week but suspicion is he'll find one or two too good again.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in fourth last week but suspicion is he'll find one or two too good again

runners.name: A Rose Adaay
runners.spotlight: Some encouragement when third of five in C&D maiden (soft) in September but beaten 10l in two subsequent handicaps; reappears as one of two for Grace Harris and it's still early days.
runners.comment: Encouraging third in C&D maiden (soft) but beaten 10l in two handicaps; still early days

runners.name: Autumn Angel
runners.spotlight: Exposed mare whose last win came in Wolverhampton classified (6f, AW) in January 2025; having first start on turf since September 2024 and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Exposed mare whose last win came in Wolverhampton classified (6f, AW) in January 2025

runners.name: Dragon God
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but he ran respectably on his last two starts, finishing third at Leicester (6f) last month and again at Windsor (5f, also good to firm; yet to race on slower than good) a fortnight ago; return to this trip can aid his cause and he looks the best of three chances for Tony Carroll.
runners.comment: Arrives in good heart and return to this trip can aid his cause too; player

runners.name: Son Of Astar
runners.spotlight: Three 5f wins on turf include his last start on grass in good-ground Haydock handicap (first-time blinkers; has won on heavy) in October; didn't quite match that in seven AW starts subsequently but now 2lb below that winning mark; placed just once from five starts at 6f and no headgear today, however.
runners.comment: Has dropped to a competitive mark but slight query tackling 6f again; headgear absent

runners.name: Alyara
runners.spotlight: Three wins, all around 1m, but the most recent was in December 2024; has been sprinting but had issues at the start on her last two outings, including when beating one home at Bath last week.
runners.comment: Issues at the start of late and beat one home at Bath last week
runners.owner: Lynn Cullimore Pete Ponting Joe Ponting

runners.name: Punchbowl Flyer
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who was also beaten a nose off this mark in September on last visit here (7f); running respectably on AW earlier this year but below par returned to turf at Haydock last month; this is easier and he's never confidently ruled out at this track.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who is on a competitive mark; not ruled out

runners.name: Captain Cess
runners.spotlight: Not fully exposed after eight starts and it was a good start handicapping when third at Bath (good to firm) and Nottingham (also about 1m, good) in April and June last year; went backwards after that (soft and AW) and now returns having his first start at 6f since 2yo days; still worth a market check.
runners.comment: Good start in this sphere but then went backwards; watch market on return/drop back to 6f

runners.name: Macarone
runners.spotlight: Four wins (one on turf) include two at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) for Ivan Furtado; tried this headgear combination for the first time when fourth back there (5f) last month and subsequently joined this yard for 2,000gns; acts with ease in the ground and nothing wrong with this mark.
runners.comment: Acts with ease in the ground and nothing wrong with this mark ahead of his stable debut

runners.name: Snuggle
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f Windsor winner (soft and good to firm), latterly in April 2024 for Luke Dace; left Claire Hobson after three starts, including fair fourth of nine at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft; first-time visor) last July; market might help on this stable debut (same owner) and first start for ten months.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins; market might help on stable debut (same owner) and first run for ten months

races.race_name: David Lucas Best Prices Welsh Sport Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Windbreaker
runners.spotlight: Two 1m AW starts in December, finishing fifth to 2,000 Guineas runner King's Trail at Kempton and third to Derby hopeful Saxon Street at Chelmsford; goes up in trip for this seasonal/turf debut and it will be disappointing if he doesn't have more to offer.
runners.comment: Two 1m AW starts in December behind colts on the Classic trail; respected on turf debut

runners.name: Battle Of Maldon
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in three Irish maidens (7f to 1m2f) on a variety of ground; well drawn and raced prominently before weakening to finish eighth of 18 at Navan (1m2f, good to yielding) four weeks ago; second, fifth and seventh have won subsequently; cheekpieces go on and he's far from discounted.
runners.comment: Showed ability in three Irish maidens; cheekpieces go on and he's not discounted

runners.name: Leonato
runners.spotlight: Some support at 11-2 before bombing out on his only start at two but much better at 20-1 when third of eight at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) on last month's reappearance; hails from the family of Dubawi and likely capable of better again; turf debut.
runners.comment: Third (1m2f, AW) on return; family of Dubawi & likely capable of better again; turf debut
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Hapiness
runners.spotlight: The sole filly; 140,000gns (fifth) foal; Camelot half-sister to winners His Majesty (5f 2yo Listed; RPR 100), Coill Avon (6f including 2yo; 93) and Spanish (1m including AW; 90); dam 6f-1m winner (including 2yo/Group 3; 104), sister to useful 1m4f/1m6f winner Billy Ray; highly respected connections so interesting to see how she goes in the market ahead of her introduction.
runners.comment: The sole filly; 140,000gns foal; respected connections & market a big help ahead of debut

runners.name: Strong Man Tom
runners.spotlight: By a miler out of a dam whose best form came at up to 1m4f so maybe bumpers weren't his thing but those two seriously heavy defeats aren't easily ignored; Flat debut.
runners.comment: By a miler but two seriously heavy defeats in bumpers aren't easily ignored; Flat debut

runners.name: Easwrith Destiny
runners.spotlight: Made his debut in a traditionally hot Newbury novice (1m2f, good; 200-1) last month; finished ninth of ten but the winner is currently the shortest-priced British contender for the Derby; unlikely to be facing anything of that calibre on this second start.
runners.comment: Made debut in hot 1m2f novice behind firm Derby hopeful; nothing of that calibre this time

races.race_name: David Lucas, Wales Leading Independent Bookmaker Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Robbo
runners.spotlight: Placed in two of his last three starts (1m2f/1m3f, AW) for this yard and he won on good to soft at Goodwood (1m) last June for the Moores; tackles stiffer opposition this time but can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Placed in two of his last three starts; tackles stiffer opposition but can't be ruled out

runners.name: The Green Mile
runners.spotlight: Bright start as 2yo in 2024 but then trailed the field in a Group 3 that October and, following an 80,000gns sale, has raced only twice for this yard, beaten 7l in one start last year and again on his comeback at Newbury 33 days ago; encouraging he's now putting a couple of runs together, though, so well worth a market check; hood added.
runners.comment: Two starts since 2024 but encouraging he's putting a couple of runs together; check market

runners.name: River King
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo; frequently up against subsequently much higher-rated rivals when in the frame first four starts then justified short odds when comfortably making all in Windsor novice (good to firm) upped to this trip in June; didn't look entirely straightforward that day so subsequent gelding operation may have sharpened his pencil and he's of obvious interest on this reappearance/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who's been gelded; mark could be lenient on reappearance/handicap debut

runners.name: Naples
runners.spotlight: 1m heavy-ground maiden winner for Paddy Twomey in Ireland; suffered heavy defeat on stable debut in Doncaster's Spring Mile in March but offered something to work on when fifth of eight in Haydock handicap (about 1m2f, good to firm) last month; unexposed after just six starts.
runners.comment: Something to work on when fifth (1m2f, good to firm) latest; unexposed after six starts

runners.name: Alcaraz
runners.spotlight: Seven-time winner in France; 0-5 for Tom Dascombe but his second at Newbury (1m2f, good to soft) in September was an eyebrow-raiser, looking all over the winner before being claimed late; considered on this stable debut/reappearance.
runners.comment: Second (1m2f, good to soft) in September was an eyebrow-raiser; stable debut/reappearance

runners.name: Crystal Mariner
runners.spotlight: 1-12 on turf; returned to form when second at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) in September then went back-to-back at Kempton (1m3f, AW) on next two starts; slowly run series final didn't work out next time; not discounted on this comeback.
runners.comment: Went back-to-back (1m3f, AW) before slowly run series final didn't work out; possibilities

races.race_name: Prix du Palais-Royal (Group 3) (Turf)

runners.name: Lazzat
runners.spotlight: Group 1 performer who retains his ability judged on his runs this season at Riyadh and Meydan; has winning form on very soft (should it ride that way by off time, Paris is due to be fairly hot today) early in his career, so should go on it, but does most of his racing on faster ground nowadays; clear chance on the figures, with Mickael Barzalona on for the first time.
runners.comment: One to beat if anywhere close to his best; Barzalona on for the first time

runners.name: Silius
runners.spotlight: Listed success over a mile at Saint-Cloud on very soft in March was a career-best and since run with credit in mile Group races at the same venue; seems to handle most ground and drop to 7f unlikely to be an issue, but isn't wearing a tongue-tie today.
runners.comment: Seems to handle most ground and drop to 7f should be okay; no tongue-tie today

runners.name: Rencontre Sportive
runners.spotlight: Won at Le Touquet in July and then over 7f on the AW at Chantilly last December but twice beaten in Listed company this year, albeit posting solid RPRs in defeat; tough task on the figures but gets the W.F.A allowance as the only 3yo in the field.
runners.comment: Tough task on the figures but gets the W.F.A allowance
runners.owner: Takumi Nakanishi, Hiroo Shimizu & Tetra United LLC

runners.name: Lanzelot Gold
runners.spotlight: Returned to something close to his best when landing a 19 runner C&D handicap in early April (good to soft) and then backed that up over C&D again in a conditions event (good); trip and ground no problem but hasn't won a Group race yet (three of his four wins have been in handicaps) and this looks a tough contest to claim a first; Cristian Demuro back on.
runners.comment: Has been running well recently but never won a Group race; Cristian Demuro back on

runners.name: Noble Champion
runners.spotlight: Held a very useful level of form after a modest debut for the fitting of a tongue-tie before surpassing all his early efforts with a 25-1 success in last year's Jersey Stakes (7f, good to firm) as a 25-1 shot; not seen since pulling up lame in the Group 2 Lennox Stakes after that, so not easy to know what to expect on his first run for 296 days.
runners.comment: Won the Jersey Stakes last year at Royal Ascot but off since finishing lame in July

races.race_name: Prix Aga Khan IV (Prix d'Ispahan) (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Daryz
runners.spotlight: Made great progress after landing a maiden for unraced colts last April here, the pinnacle being his win in the Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe (1m4f, very soft) here in October, finishing ahead of two of these, and not far below that level when handling the drop in trip for the Prix Ganay (1m2f110yds, good) on his recent return to action; goes on any ground and sets the standard, with the even shorter distance unlikely to cause him any issues considering his closing sectionals 25 days ago.
runners.comment: Won last year's Arc, finishing a head of a couple of these, and showed speed on his return

runners.name: Sosie
runners.spotlight: Top-class performer who won this race last year as an odds-on shot and went on to have a solid campaign, albeit third behind Daryz in the Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe when that rival was getting weight for age; won a 1m4f Group 1 in Sha Tin after on good (also career-best) and heads into this off a fine third at the same venue in a 1m2f Group 1 on good to firm; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Won this last year and generally a solid type at the highest level; hard to dismiss

runners.name: Leffard
runners.spotlight: Undoubtedly classy on his day and won his Group 1 in last July's Grand Prix de Paris here (1m4f, good to soft) before excuses could be made for his run behind Daryz in the Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe; produced an eyecatching effort in the Group 2 Prix d'Harcourt (1m2f, good to soft) here at the start of April on his 4yo return and should play a part if this drop in distance doesn't prove against him.
runners.comment: Undoubtedly classy on his day and produced good seasonal return; should play a part

runners.name: Qilin Queen
runners.spotlight: Progressive filly last term, landing a Listed event at Newbury (1m2f, good to firm) before enjoying her finest hour when landing the Group 2 Prix de Malleret (1m4f, good to soft) here last July, but form dropped off in Group 1s after, the most recent a tenth of 12 in the 1m2f Prix de l'Opera here as a 50-1 shot in October; plenty to find on her return up against some race-fit colts.
runners.comment: Won a Group 2 as a 3yo from a top prospect but this is a hard start for her 4yo season

runners.name: Divine Chrisnat
runners.spotlight: Won eight of her 51 starts, of which six were handicaps; has placed in Listed company but absolutely no chance of landing this on anything she has shown so far.
runners.comment: No chance of winning this on anything she has done so far

races.race_name: Prix Vicomtesse Vigier (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Santorini Star
runners.spotlight: Dual Group 2 winner who has a good strike-rate, though came up short behind Consent on sole outing on very soft ground in the Royallieu on Arc weekend; showed she retains her ability when winning the Jockey Club Stakes on reappearance but she'll have to fare better to win back in Group 1 company.
runners.comment: Ran to best when winning Group 2 at Newmarket on reappearance; ground a bit of a worry

runners.name: Al Riffa
runners.spotlight: Last year's Irish St Leger winner who ran respectably in the Melbourne Cup and when fourth in a falsely run Hong Kong Vase; disappointing in the Dubai Gold Cup in March and still to prove stamina over this trip on testing ground; cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Disappointing in the Dubai Gold Cup in March; still to prove 2m on testing ground suits

runners.name: Caballo De Mar
runners.spotlight: Last year's Cadran winner (2m4f, very soft) but, although behind Arrow Eagle less than two weeks later, that run may have come too quickly, has run well on both starts this year in the Dubai Gold Cup and Sagaro (career-best effort) and he'll be suited by the return to this ground.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner in October and has run well both starts this year; leading claims

runners.name: Consent
runners.spotlight: Late-developing filly who turned in her best effort when reversing Park Hill form with Santorini Star in the Group 1 Royallieu on Arc weekend, keeping on in a manner that suggested she'll stay this longer trip; hasn't been out since but likely to be fit enough to do herself justice; fascinatingi runner.
runners.comment: Turned in a clear pb when winning Group 1 Royallieu on Arc day; fascinating runner

runners.name: Fairy Glen
runners.spotlight: Improved performer this year, bagging a pair of Group 2 events at Meydan, most recently in the Dubai Gold Cup, where she had too much in the way of pace for Caballo De Mar; may not confirm those placings with that rival on this testing surface, though.
runners.comment: Best effort to win at Meydan on World Cup night but more to do back on testing ground
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Arrow Eagle
runners.spotlight: Group 1 Royal-Oak winner over this trip on testing ground and shaped as though retaining much of ability in a race that didn't play to his strengths behind the top-class Daryz over an inadequate trip in the Ganay on reappearance; has plenty in his favour this time.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner over this trip in autumn and creditable reappearance over inadequate trip

runners.name: Double Major
runners.spotlight: Dual Group 1 winner who has shown he retains all his ability this year (closely matched with Asmarani); bit to find with Arrow Eagle on Royal-Oak form and will have to raise game if he's to win this.

runners.name: Asmarani
runners.spotlight: Has won half of eight starts and has been an improved performer since being gelded, beating Double Major on each of last two starts over 1m7f; should be close between the pair at these weights but he'll have to step up again to extend the winning sequence; yard won this last year.
runners.comment: Improved performer this year and should give another good account

runners.name: Rashford
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner over 1m4f in Germany in autumn and ran as well as he's ever done when third on reappearance/first run for Tim Donworth in Listed event at Saint-Cloud in March; has stamina to prove over this trip, though.
runners.comment: Ran well on first run for yard in March but stamina to prove over this trip
